{{Infobox ancient site |name = Maezuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maezuka Kofun (Takatsuki), zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the tumulus |Group = |location = Okamoto-chō 33 (Azatsuka-no-Mae), {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 51|Latitude seconds = 8.71 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 35|Longitude seconds = 33.35 |ISO = JP-27 |Type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |Dimensions = Tumulus length: 94 m |Construction = 5th century |Burial Facility = Unknown (contains a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺}} made of tuff) |Buried Individual = Unknown |Artifacts = Grave goods, haniwa fragments |Historic Site = None |Cultural Property = Sarcophagus (Osaka Prefecture-designated cultural property) |Notes = |Map = Japan Osaka |Label = Maezuka Kofun |Label position = top }} '''Maezuka Kofun''' (前塚古墳) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} located in {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The Maezuka Kofun was constructed at the southern end of the Bentenyama Hills in northern Takatsuki City, Osaka Prefecture. Over time, the mound has been partially leveled due to agricultural activities, and the surrounding moat has been interrupted by the construction of a road on the south side. Archaeological investigations have uncovered artifacts and remains of the tumulus, including a sarcophagus unearthed during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a short front section (a defining feature of this type of kofun) oriented westward. Fragments of haniwa, including cylindrical, lid-shaped, and house-shaped examples, have been found on the surface of the mound. The surrounding moat is 10–17 meters wide, and the total length of the kofun, including the moat, is estimated to be 120 meters. Although the specific structure of the burial facility is unclear, it is known that a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺}} made of tuff was found inside, along with grave goods. Based on the haniwa fragments and sarcophagus design, the Maezuka Kofun is estimated to have been built during the 5th century, placing it in the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Previously, it was believed to be an {{ill|Baizuka|lt=accompanying mound|ja|陪塚}} of the nearby {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}}, but current research classifies it as an earlier kofun. The sarcophagus was designated as an Osaka Prefecture-designated tangible cultural property in 1974. == Dimensions == The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length (including moat): 120 meters * Tumulus length: 94 meters * Rear section (circular): ** Diameter: 69 meters * Front section (rectangular): ** Length: 25 meters ** Width: 25 meters (estimated) The construction plan shows similarities to the nearby {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳}} in Takatsuki City. The surrounding moat mirrors the shape of the tumulus, measuring 16 meters wide on the rear side and 10 meters on the front side. == Burial Facility == [[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun
Designated as an Osaka Prefecture cultural property}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] The burial facility's structure is unknown, but it contained a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺}}, which was exposed and discovered during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The sarcophagus, made of tuff, measures approximately 2 meters in length, 0.6 meters in width, and 0.8 meters in height. Its lid features a curved shape (so-called "Inrō-lid"), with rope-like protrusions on each side and triangular incisions on the front and back ends. Grave goods such as mirrors, iron swords, and iron halberds were reportedly found, although their details remain unclear. The sarcophagus has been preserved and is designated as an Osaka Prefecture-designated tangible cultural property. The original sarcophagus is displayed at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while a replica is exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校}}. == Cultural Property == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property: ** Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun (archaeological artifact) - Preserved at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]. Full view of the tumulus |Group = |location = Okamoto-chō 33 (Azatsuka-no-Mae), {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 51|Latitude seconds = 8.71 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 35|Longitude seconds = 33.35 |ISO = JP-27 |Type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |Dimensions = Tumulus length: 94 m |Construction = 5th century |Burial Facility = Unknown (contains a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺}} made of tuff) |Buried Individual = Unknown |Artifacts = Grave goods, haniwa fragments |Historic Site = None |Cultural Property = Sarcophagus (Osaka Prefecture-designated cultural property) |Notes = |Map = Japan Osaka |Label = Maezuka Kofun |Label position = top }} '''Maezuka Kofun''' (前塚古墳) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} located in {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The Maezuka Kofun was constructed at the southern end of the Bentenyama Hills in northern Takatsuki City, Osaka Prefecture. Over time, the mound has been partially leveled due to agricultural activities, and the surrounding moat has been interrupted by the construction of a road on the south side. Archaeological investigations have uncovered artifacts and remains of the tumulus, including a sarcophagus unearthed during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a short front section (a defining feature of this type of kofun) oriented westward. Fragments of haniwa, including cylindrical, lid-shaped, and house-shaped examples, have been found on the surface of the mound. The surrounding moat is 10–17 meters wide, and the total length of the kofun, including the moat, is estimated to be 120 meters. Although the specific structure of the burial facility is unclear, it is known that a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺}} made of tuff was found inside, along with grave goods. Based on the haniwa fragments and sarcophagus design, the Maezuka Kofun is estimated to have been built during the 5th century, placing it in the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Previously, it was believed to be an {{ill|Baizuka|lt=accompanying mound|ja|陪塚}} of the nearby {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}}, but current research classifies it as an earlier kofun. The sarcophagus was designated as an Osaka Prefecture-designated tangible cultural property in 1974. == Dimensions == The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length (including moat): 120 meters * Tumulus length: 94 meters * Rear section (circular): ** Diameter: 69 meters * Front section (rectangular): ** Length: 25 meters ** Width: 25 meters (estimated) The construction plan shows similarities to the nearby {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳}} in Takatsuki City. The surrounding moat mirrors the shape of the tumulus, measuring 16 meters wide on the rear side and 10 meters on the front side. == Burial Facility == [[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun
Designated as an Osaka Prefecture cultural property}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] The burial facility's structure is unknown, but it contained a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺}}, which was exposed and discovered during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The sarcophagus, made of tuff, measures approximately 2 meters in length, 0.6 meters in width, and 0.8 meters in height. Its lid features a curved shape (so-called "Inrō-lid"), with rope-like protrusions on each side and triangular incisions on the front and back ends. Grave goods such as mirrors, iron swords, and iron halberds were reportedly found, although their details remain unclear. The sarcophagus has been preserved and is designated as an Osaka Prefecture-designated tangible cultural property. The original sarcophagus is displayed at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while a replica is exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校}}. == Cultural Property == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property: ** Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun (archaeological artifact) - Preserved at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property List] (Osaka Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] ({{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, Minamikawachi District) - Preserves and exhibits the original sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校}} ({{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Shinjōchō) - Displays a replica of the sarcophagus. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Excavations, No. 15)]|year=1991|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=20–26}} * Other sources: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本歴史地名大系 28 大阪府の地名 (Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place names, Volume 28, Osaka)|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本古墳大辞典 (Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun)|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989}} == {{Infobox ancient site |name = Maezuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maezuka Kofun (Takatsuki), zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the tumulus |Group = |location = Okamoto-chō 33 (Azatsuka-no-Mae), {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 51|Latitude seconds = 8.71 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 35|Longitude seconds = 33.35 |ISO = JP-27 |Type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |Dimensions = Tumulus length: 94 m |Construction = 5th century |Burial Facility = Unknown (contains a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺}} made of tuff) |Buried Individual = Unknown |Artifacts = Grave goods, haniwa fragments |Historic Site = None |Cultural Property = Sarcophagus (Osaka Prefecture-designated cultural property) |Notes = |Map = Japan Osaka |Label = Maezuka Kofun |Label position = top }} '''Maezuka Kofun''' (前塚古墳) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} located in {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The Maezuka Kofun was constructed at the southern end of the Bentenyama Hills in northern Takatsuki City, Osaka Prefecture. Over time, the mound has been partially leveled due to agricultural activities, and the surrounding moat has been interrupted by the construction of a road on the south side. Archaeological investigations have uncovered artifacts and remains of the tumulus, including a sarcophagus unearthed during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a short front section (a defining feature of this type of kofun) oriented westward. Fragments of haniwa, including cylindrical, lid-shaped, and house-shaped examples, have been found on the surface of the mound. The surrounding moat is 10–17 meters wide, and the total length of the kofun, including the moat, is estimated to be 120 meters. Although the specific structure of the burial facility is unclear, it is known that a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺}} made of tuff was found inside, along with grave goods. Based on the haniwa fragments and sarcophagus design, the Maezuka Kofun is estimated to have been built during the 5th century, placing it in the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Previously, it was believed to be an {{ill|Baizuka|lt=accompanying mound|ja|陪塚}} of the nearby {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}}, but current research classifies it as an earlier kofun. The sarcophagus was designated as an Osaka Prefecture-designated tangible cultural property in 1974. == Dimensions == The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length (including moat): 120 meters * Tumulus length: 94 meters * Rear section (circular): ** Diameter: 69 meters * Front section (rectangular): ** Length: 25 meters ** Width: 25 meters (estimated) The construction plan shows similarities to the nearby {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳}} in Takatsuki City. The surrounding moat mirrors the shape of the tumulus, measuring 16 meters wide on the rear side and 10 meters on the front side. == Burial Facility == [[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun
Designated as an Osaka Prefecture cultural property}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] The burial facility's structure is unknown, but it contained a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺}}, which was exposed and discovered during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The sarcophagus, made of tuff, measures approximately 2 meters in length, 0.6 meters in width, and 0.8 meters in height. Its lid features a curved shape (so-called "Inrō-lid"), with rope-like protrusions on each side and triangular incisions on the front and back ends. Grave goods such as mirrors, iron swords, and iron halberds were reportedly found, although their details remain unclear. The sarcophagus has been preserved and is designated as an Osaka Prefecture-designated tangible cultural property. The original sarcophagus is displayed at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while a replica is exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校}}. == Cultural Property == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property: ** Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun (archaeological artifact) - Preserved at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property List] (Osaka Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] ({{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, Minamikawachi District) - Preserves and exhibits the original sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校}} ({{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Shinjōchō) - Displays a replica of the sarcophagus. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Local government publications: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=高槻市史 第6巻 考古編 (Takatsuki City History, Volume 6, Archaeological Edition)|year=1973|publisher=Takatsuki City|pages=59–60}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4900 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要9 (Summary of Excavations, No. 9)]|year=1985|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|page=18}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4644 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要13 (Summary of Excavations, No. 13)]|year=1989|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=19–23}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/5175 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要15 (Summary of Excavations, No. 15)]|year=1991|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=20–26}} * Other sources: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本歴史地名大系 28 大阪府の地名 (Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place names, Volume 28, Osaka)|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本古墳大辞典 (Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun)|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989}} == Fragments of haniwa, including cylindrical, lid-shaped, and house-shaped examples, have been found on the surface of the mound. The surrounding moat is 10–17 meters wide, and the total length of the kofun, including the moat, is estimated to be 120 meters. Although the specific structure of the burial facility is unclear, it is known that a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺}} made of tuff was found inside, along with grave goods. Based on the haniwa fragments and sarcophagus design, the Maezuka Kofun is estimated to have been built during the 5th century, placing it in the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Previously, it was believed to be an {{ill|Baizuka|lt=accompanying mound|ja|陪塚}} of the nearby {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}}, but current research classifies it as an earlier kofun. The sarcophagus was designated as an Osaka Prefecture-designated tangible cultural property in 1974. == Dimensions == The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length (including moat): 120 meters * Tumulus length: 94 meters * Rear section (circular): ** Diameter: 69 meters * Front section (rectangular): ** Length: 25 meters ** Width: 25 meters (estimated) The construction plan shows similarities to the nearby {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳}} in Takatsuki City. The surrounding moat mirrors the shape of the tumulus, measuring 16 meters wide on the rear side and 10 meters on the front side. == Burial Facility == [[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun
Designated as an Osaka Prefecture cultural property}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] The burial facility's structure is unknown, but it contained a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺}}, which was exposed and discovered during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The sarcophagus, made of tuff, measures approximately 2 meters in length, 0.6 meters in width, and 0.8 meters in height. Its lid features a curved shape (so-called "Inrō-lid"), with rope-like protrusions on each side and triangular incisions on the front and back ends. Grave goods such as mirrors, iron swords, and iron halberds were reportedly found, although their details remain unclear. The sarcophagus has been preserved and is designated as an Osaka Prefecture-designated tangible cultural property. The original sarcophagus is displayed at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while a replica is exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校}}. == Cultural Property == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property: ** Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun (archaeological artifact) - Preserved at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property List] (Osaka Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] ({{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, Minamikawachi District) - Preserves and exhibits the original sarcophagus from {{Infobox ancient site |name = Maezuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maezuka Kofun (Takatsuki), zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the tumulus |Group = |location = Okamoto-chō 33 (Azatsuka-no-Mae), {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 51|Latitude seconds = 8.71 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 35|Longitude seconds = 33.35 |ISO = JP-27 |Type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |Dimensions = Tumulus length: 94 m |Construction = 5th century |Burial Facility = Unknown (contains a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺}} made of tuff) |Buried Individual = Unknown |Artifacts = Grave goods, haniwa fragments |Historic Site = None |Cultural Property = Sarcophagus (Osaka Prefecture-designated cultural property) |Notes = |Map = Japan Osaka |Label = Maezuka Kofun |Label position = top }} '''Maezuka Kofun''' (前塚古墳) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} located in {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The Maezuka Kofun was constructed at the southern end of the Bentenyama Hills in northern Takatsuki City, Osaka Prefecture. Over time, the mound has been partially leveled due to agricultural activities, and the surrounding moat has been interrupted by the construction of a road on the south side. Archaeological investigations have uncovered artifacts and remains of the tumulus, including a sarcophagus unearthed during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a short front section (a defining feature of this type of kofun) oriented westward. Fragments of haniwa, including cylindrical, lid-shaped, and house-shaped examples, have been found on the surface of the mound. The surrounding moat is 10–17 meters wide, and the total length of the kofun, including the moat, is estimated to be 120 meters. Although the specific structure of the burial facility is unclear, it is known that a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺}} made of tuff was found inside, along with grave goods. Based on the haniwa fragments and sarcophagus design, the Maezuka Kofun is estimated to have been built during the 5th century, placing it in the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Previously, it was believed to be an {{ill|Baizuka|lt=accompanying mound|ja|陪塚}} of the nearby {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}}, but current research classifies it as an earlier kofun. The sarcophagus was designated as an Osaka Prefecture-designated tangible cultural property in 1974. == Dimensions == The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length (including moat): 120 meters * Tumulus length: 94 meters * Rear section (circular): ** Diameter: 69 meters * Front section (rectangular): ** Length: 25 meters ** Width: 25 meters (estimated) The construction plan shows similarities to the nearby {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳}} in Takatsuki City. The surrounding moat mirrors the shape of the tumulus, measuring 16 meters wide on the rear side and 10 meters on the front side. == Burial Facility == [[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun
Designated as an Osaka Prefecture cultural property}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] The burial facility's structure is unknown, but it contained a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺}}, which was exposed and discovered during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The sarcophagus, made of tuff, measures approximately 2 meters in length, 0.6 meters in width, and 0.8 meters in height. Its lid features a curved shape (so-called "Inrō-lid"), with rope-like protrusions on each side and triangular incisions on the front and back ends. Grave goods such as mirrors, iron swords, and iron halberds were reportedly found, although their details remain unclear. The sarcophagus has been preserved and is designated as an Osaka Prefecture-designated tangible cultural property. The original sarcophagus is displayed at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while a replica is exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校}}. == Cultural Property == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property: ** Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun (archaeological artifact) - Preserved at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property List] (Osaka Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] ({{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, Minamikawachi District) - Preserves and exhibits the original sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校}} ({{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Shinjōchō) - Displays a replica of the sarcophagus. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Local government publications: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=高槻市史 第6巻 考古編 (Takatsuki City History, Volume 6, Archaeological Edition)|year=1973|publisher=Takatsuki City|pages=59–60}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4900 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要9 (Summary of Excavations, No. 9)]|year=1985|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|page=18}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4644 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要13 (Summary of Excavations, No. 13)]|year=1989|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=19–23}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/5175 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要15 (Summary of Excavations, No. 15)]|year=1991|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=20–26}} * Other sources: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本歴史地名大系 28 大阪府の地名 (Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place names, Volume 28, Osaka)|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本古墳大辞典 (Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun)|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989}} == The sarcophagus was designated as an Osaka Prefecture-designated tangible cultural property in 1974. == Dimensions == The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length (including moat): 120 meters * Tumulus length: 94 meters * Rear section (circular): ** Diameter: 69 meters * Front section (rectangular): ** Length: 25 meters ** Width: 25 meters (estimated) The construction plan shows similarities to the nearby {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳}} in Takatsuki City. The surrounding moat mirrors the shape of the tumulus, measuring 16 meters wide on the rear side and 10 meters on the front side. == Burial Facility == [[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun
Designated as an Osaka Prefecture cultural property}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] The burial facility's structure is unknown, but it contained a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺}}, which was exposed and discovered during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The sarcophagus, made of tuff, measures approximately 2 meters in length, 0.6 meters in width, and 0.8 meters in height. Its lid features a curved shape (so-called "Inrō-lid"), with rope-like protrusions on each side and triangular incisions on the front and back ends. Grave goods such as mirrors, iron swords, and iron halberds were reportedly found, although their details remain unclear. The sarcophagus has been preserved and is designated as an Osaka Prefecture-designated tangible cultural property. The original sarcophagus is displayed at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while a replica is exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校}}. == Cultural Property == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property: ** Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun (archaeological artifact) - Preserved at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property List] (Osaka Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] ({{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, Minamikawachi District) - Preserves and exhibits the original sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校}} ({{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Shinjōchō) - Displays a replica of the sarcophagus. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further The tumulus is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The Maezuka Kofun was constructed at the southern end of the Bentenyama Hills in northern Takatsuki City, Osaka Prefecture. Previously, it was believed to be an {{ill|Baizuka|lt=accompanying mound|ja|陪塚}} of the nearby {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}}, but current research classifies it as an earlier kofun. revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Maezuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maezuka Kofun (Takatsuki), zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the tumulus |Group = |location = Okamoto-chō 33 (Azatsuka-no-Mae), {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 51|Latitude seconds = 8.71 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 35|Longitude seconds = 33.35 |ISO = JP-27 |Type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |Dimensions = Tumulus length: 94 m |Construction = 5th century |Burial Facility = Unknown (contains a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺}} made of tuff) |Buried Individual = Unknown |Artifacts = Grave goods, haniwa fragments |Historic Site = None |Cultural Property = Sarcophagus (Osaka Prefecture-designated cultural property) |Notes = |Map = Japan Osaka |Label = Maezuka Kofun |Label position = top }} '''Maezuka Kofun''' (前塚古墳) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} located in {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The Maezuka Kofun was constructed at the southern end of the Bentenyama Hills in northern Takatsuki City, Osaka Prefecture. Over time, the mound has been partially leveled due to agricultural activities, and the surrounding moat has been interrupted by the construction of a road on the south side. Archaeological investigations have uncovered artifacts and remains of the tumulus, including a sarcophagus unearthed during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a short front section (a defining feature of this type of kofun) oriented westward. Fragments of haniwa, including cylindrical, lid-shaped, and house-shaped examples, have been found on the surface of the mound. The surrounding moat is 10–17 meters wide, and the total length of the kofun, including the moat, is estimated to be 120 meters. Although the specific structure of the burial facility is unclear, it is known that a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺}} made of tuff was found inside, along with grave goods. Based on the haniwa fragments and sarcophagus design, the Maezuka Kofun is estimated to have been built during the 5th century, placing it in the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Previously, it was believed to be an {{ill|Baizuka|lt=accompanying mound|ja|陪塚}} of the nearby {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}}, but current research classifies it as an earlier kofun. The sarcophagus was designated as an Osaka Prefecture-designated tangible cultural property in 1974. == Dimensions == The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length (including moat): 120 meters * Tumulus length: 94 meters * Rear section (circular): ** Diameter: 69 meters * Front section (rectangular): ** Length: 25 meters ** Width: 25 meters (estimated) The construction plan shows similarities to the nearby {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳}} in Takatsuki City. The surrounding moat mirrors the shape of the tumulus, measuring 16 meters wide on the rear side and 10 meters on the front side. == Burial Facility == [[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun
Designated as an Osaka Prefecture cultural property}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] The burial facility's structure is unknown, but it contained a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺}}, which was exposed and discovered during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The sarcophagus, made of tuff, measures approximately 2 meters in length, 0.6 meters in width, and 0.8 meters in height. Its lid features a curved shape (so-called "Inrō-lid"), with rope-like protrusions on each side and triangular incisions on the front and back ends. Grave goods such as mirrors, iron swords, and iron halberds were reportedly found, although their details remain unclear. The sarcophagus has been preserved and is designated as an Osaka Prefecture-designated tangible cultural property. The original sarcophagus is displayed at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while a replica is exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校}}. == Cultural Property == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property: ** Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun (archaeological artifact) - Preserved at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property List] (Osaka Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] ({{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, Minamikawachi District) - Preserves and exhibits the original sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校}} ({{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Shinjōchō) - Displays a replica of the sarcophagus. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Local government publications: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=高槻市史 第6巻 考古編 (Takatsuki City History, Volume 6, Archaeological Edition)|year=1973|publisher=Takatsuki City|pages=59–60}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4900 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要9 (Summary of Excavations, No. 9)]|year=1985|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|page=18}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4644 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要13 (Summary of Excavations, No. 13)]|year=1989|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=19–23}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/5175 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要15 (Summary of Excavations, No. 15)]|year=1991|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=20–26}} * Other sources: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本歴史地名大系 28 大阪府の地名 (Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place names, Volume 28, Osaka)|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本古墳大辞典 (Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun)|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Maetsuka Kofun-qq]] The sarcophagus was designated as an Osaka Prefecture-designated tangible cultural property in 1974. == Dimensions == The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length (including moat): 120 meters * Tumulus length: 94 meters * Rear section (circular): ** Diameter: 69 meters * Front section (rectangular): ** Length: 25 meters ** Width: 25 meters (estimated) The construction plan shows similarities to the nearby {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳}} in Takatsuki City. The surrounding moat mirrors the shape of the tumulus, measuring 16 meters wide on the rear side and 10 meters on the front side. == Burial Facility == [[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun
Designated as an Osaka Prefecture cultural property}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] The burial facility's structure is unknown, but it contained a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺}}, which was exposed and discovered during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The sarcophagus, made of tuff, measures approximately 2 meters in length, 0.6 meters in width, and 0.8 meters in height. Its lid features a curved shape (so-called "Inrō-lid"), with rope-like protrusions on each side and triangular incisions on the front and back ends. Grave goods such as mirrors, iron swords, and iron halberds were reportedly found, although their details remain unclear. The sarcophagus has been preserved and is designated as an Osaka Prefecture-designated tangible cultural property. The original sarcophagus is displayed at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while a replica is exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校}}. == Cultural Property == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property: ** Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun (archaeological artifact) - Preserved at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property List] (Osaka Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] ({{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, Minamikawachi District) - Preserves and exhibits the original sarcophagus from The tumulus is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The Maezuka Kofun was constructed at the southern end of the Bentenyama Hills in northern Takatsuki City, Osaka Prefecture. Previously, it was believed to be an {{ill|Baizuka|lt=accompanying mound|ja|陪塚}} of the nearby {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}}, but current research classifies it as an earlier kofun. revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Maezuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maezuka Kofun (Takatsuki), zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the tumulus |Group = |location = Okamoto-chō 33 (Azatsuka-no-Mae), {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 51|Latitude seconds = 8.71 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 35|Longitude seconds = 33.35 |ISO = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |Dimensions = Tumulus length: 94 m |Construction = 5th century |Burial Facility = Unknown (contains a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} made of tuff) |Buried Individual = Unknown |Artifacts = Grave goods, haniwa fragments |Historic Site = None |Cultural Property = Sarcophagus (Osaka Prefecture-designated cultural property) |Notes = |Map = Japan Osaka |Label = Maezuka Kofun |Label position = top }} '''Maezuka Kofun''' (前塚古墳) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The Maezuka Kofun was constructed at the southern end of the Bentenyama Hills in northern Takatsuki City, Osaka Prefecture. Over time, the mound has been partially leveled due to agricultural activities, and the surrounding moat has been interrupted by the construction of a road on the south side. Archaeological investigations have uncovered artifacts and remains of the tumulus, including a sarcophagus unearthed during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a short front section (a defining feature of this type of kofun) oriented westward. Fragments of haniwa, including cylindrical, lid-shaped, and house-shaped examples, have been found on the surface of the mound. The surrounding moat is 10–17 meters wide, and the total length of the kofun, including the moat, is estimated to be 120 meters. Although the specific structure of the burial facility is unclear, it is known that a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} made of tuff was found inside, along with grave goods. Based on the haniwa fragments and sarcophagus design, the Maezuka Kofun is estimated to have been built during the 5th century, placing it in the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Previously, it was believed to be an {{ill|Baizuka|lt=accompanying mound|ja|陪塚}} of the nearby {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}}, but current research classifies it as an earlier kofun. The sarcophagus was designated as an Osaka Prefecture-designated tangible cultural property in 1974. == Dimensions == The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length (including moat): 120 meters * Tumulus length: 94 meters * Rear section (circular): ** Diameter: 69 meters * Front section (rectangular): ** Length: 25 meters ** Width: 25 meters (estimated) The construction plan shows similarities to the nearby {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳}} in Takatsuki City. The surrounding moat mirrors the shape of the tumulus, measuring 16 meters wide on the rear side and 10 meters on the front side. == Burial Facility == [[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun
Designated as an Osaka Prefecture cultural property}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] The burial facility's structure is unknown, but it contained a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}, which was exposed and discovered during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The sarcophagus, made of tuff, measures approximately 2 meters in length, 0.6 meters in width, and 0.8 meters in height. Its lid features a curved shape (so-called "Inrō-lid"), with rope-like protrusions on each side and triangular incisions on the front and back ends. Grave goods such as mirrors, iron swords, and iron halberds were reportedly found, although their details remain unclear. The sarcophagus has been preserved and is designated as an Osaka Prefecture-designated tangible cultural property. The original sarcophagus is displayed at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while a replica is exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校}}. == Cultural Property == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property: ** Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun (archaeological artifact) - Preserved at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property List] (Osaka Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] ({{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, Minamikawachi District) - Preserves and exhibits the original sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校}} ({{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Shinjōchō) - Displays a replica of the sarcophagus. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Local government publications: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=高槻市史 第6巻 考古編 (Takatsuki City History, Volume 6, Archaeological Edition)|year=1973|publisher=Takatsuki City|pages=59–60}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4900 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要9 (Summary of Excavations, No. 9)]|year=1985|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|page=18}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4644 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要13 (Summary of Excavations, No. 13)]|year=1989|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=19–23}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/5175 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要15 (Summary of Excavations, No. 15)]|year=1991|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=20–26}} * Other sources: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本歴史地名大系 28 大阪府の地名 (Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place names, Volume 28, Osaka)|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本古墳大辞典 (Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun)|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Maetsuka Kofun-qq]] Full view of the tumulus |Group = |location = Okamoto-chō 33 (Azatsuka-no-Mae), {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 51|Latitude seconds = 8.71 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 35|Longitude seconds = 33.35 |ISO = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |Dimensions = Tumulus length: 94 m |Construction = 5th century |Burial Facility = Unknown (contains a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} made of tuff) |Buried Individual = Unknown |Artifacts = Grave goods, haniwa fragments |Historic Site = None |Cultural Property = Sarcophagus (Osaka Prefecture-designated cultural property) |Notes = |Map = Japan Osaka |Label = Maezuka Kofun |Label position = top }} '''Maezuka Kofun''' (前塚古墳) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The Maezuka Kofun was constructed at the southern end of the Bentenyama Hills in northern Takatsuki City, Osaka Prefecture. Over time, the mound has been partially leveled due to agricultural activities, and the surrounding moat has been interrupted by the construction of a road on the south side. Archaeological investigations have uncovered artifacts and remains of the tumulus, including a sarcophagus unearthed during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a short front section (a defining feature of this type of kofun) oriented westward. Fragments of haniwa, including cylindrical, lid-shaped, and house-shaped examples, have been found on the surface of the mound. The surrounding moat is 10–17 meters wide, and the total length of the kofun, including the moat, is estimated to be 120 meters. Although the specific structure of the burial facility is unclear, it is known that a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} made of tuff was found inside, along with grave goods. Based on the haniwa fragments and sarcophagus design, the Maezuka Kofun is estimated to have been built during the 5th century, placing it in the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Previously, it was believed to be an {{ill|Baizuka|lt=accompanying mound|ja|陪塚}} of the nearby {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}}, but current research classifies it as an earlier kofun. The sarcophagus was designated as an Osaka Prefecture-designated tangible cultural property in 1974. == Dimensions == The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length (including moat): 120 meters * Tumulus length: 94 meters * Rear section (circular): ** Diameter: 69 meters * Front section (rectangular): ** Length: 25 meters ** Width: 25 meters (estimated) The construction plan shows similarities to the nearby {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳}} in Takatsuki City. The surrounding moat mirrors the shape of the tumulus, measuring 16 meters wide on the rear side and 10 meters on the front side. == Burial Facility == [[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun
Designated as an Osaka Prefecture cultural property}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] The burial facility's structure is unknown, but it contained a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}, which was exposed and discovered during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The sarcophagus, made of tuff, measures approximately 2 meters in length, 0.6 meters in width, and 0.8 meters in height. Its lid features a curved shape (so-called "Inrō-lid"), with rope-like protrusions on each side and triangular incisions on the front and back ends. Grave goods such as mirrors, iron swords, and iron halberds were reportedly found, although their details remain unclear. The sarcophagus has been preserved and is designated as an Osaka Prefecture-designated tangible cultural property. The original sarcophagus is displayed at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while a replica is exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校}}. == Cultural Property == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property: ** Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun (archaeological artifact) - Preserved at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property List] (Osaka Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] ({{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, Minamikawachi District) - Preserves and exhibits the original sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校}} ({{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Shinjōchō) - Displays a replica of the sarcophagus. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property List] (Osaka Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] ({{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, Minamikawachi District) - Preserves and exhibits the original sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校}} ({{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Shinjōchō) - Displays a replica of the sarcophagus. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Local government publications: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=高槻市史 第6巻 考古編 (Takatsuki City History, Volume 6, Archaeological Edition)|year=1973|publisher=Takatsuki {{Infobox ancient site |name = Maezuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maezuka Kofun (Takatsuki), zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the tumulus |Group = |location = Okamoto-chō 33 (Azatsuka-no-Mae), {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 51|Latitude seconds = 8.71 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 35|Longitude seconds = 33.35 |ISO = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |Dimensions = Tumulus length: 94 m |Construction = 5th century |Burial Facility = Unknown (contains a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} made of tuff) |Buried Individual = Unknown |Artifacts = Grave goods, haniwa fragments |Historic Site = None |Cultural Property = Sarcophagus (Osaka Prefecture-designated cultural property) |Notes = |Map = Japan Osaka |Label = Maezuka Kofun |Label position = top }} '''Maezuka Kofun''' (前塚古墳) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The Maezuka Kofun was constructed at the southern end of the Bentenyama Hills in northern Takatsuki City, Osaka Prefecture. Over time, the mound has been partially leveled due to agricultural activities, and the surrounding moat has been interrupted by the construction of a road on the south side. Archaeological investigations have uncovered artifacts and remains of the tumulus, including a sarcophagus unearthed during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a short front section (a defining feature of this type of kofun) oriented westward. Fragments of haniwa, including cylindrical, lid-shaped, and house-shaped examples, have been found on the surface of the mound. The surrounding moat is 10–17 meters wide, and the total length of the kofun, including the moat, is estimated to be 120 meters. Although the specific structure of the burial facility is unclear, it is known that a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} made of tuff was found inside, along with grave goods. Based on the haniwa fragments and sarcophagus design, the Maezuka Kofun is estimated to have been built during the 5th century, placing it in the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Previously, it was believed to be an {{ill|Baizuka|lt=accompanying mound|ja|陪塚}} of the nearby {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}}, but current research classifies it as an earlier kofun. The sarcophagus was designated as an Osaka Prefecture-designated tangible cultural property in 1974. == Dimensions == The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length (including moat): 120 meters * Tumulus length: 94 meters * Rear section (circular): ** Diameter: 69 meters * Front section (rectangular): ** Length: 25 meters ** Width: 25 meters (estimated) The construction plan shows similarities to the nearby {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳}} in Takatsuki City. The surrounding moat mirrors the shape of the tumulus, measuring 16 meters wide on the rear side and 10 meters on the front side. == Burial Facility == [[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun
Designated as an Osaka Prefecture cultural property}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] The burial facility's structure is unknown, but it contained a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}, which was exposed and discovered during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The sarcophagus, made of tuff, measures approximately 2 meters in length, 0.6 meters in width, and 0.8 meters in height. Its lid features a curved shape (so-called "Inrō-lid"), with rope-like protrusions on each side and triangular incisions on the front and back ends. Grave goods such as mirrors, iron swords, and iron halberds were reportedly found, although their details remain unclear. The sarcophagus has been preserved and is designated as an Osaka Prefecture-designated tangible cultural property. The original sarcophagus is displayed at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while a replica is exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校}}. == Cultural Property == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property: ** Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun (archaeological artifact) - Preserved at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property List] (Osaka Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] ({{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, Minamikawachi District) - Preserves and exhibits the original sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校}} ({{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Shinjōchō) - Displays a replica of the sarcophagus. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Local government publications: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=高槻市史 第6巻 考古編 (Takatsuki City History, Volume 6, Archaeological Edition)|year=1973|publisher=Takatsuki City|pages=59–60}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4900 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要9 (Summary of Excavations, No. 9)]|year=1985|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|page=18}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4644 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要13 (Summary of Excavations, No. 13)]|year=1989|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=19–23}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/5175 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要15 (Summary of Excavations, No. 15)]|year=1991|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=20–26}} * Other sources: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本歴史地名大系 28 大阪府の地名 (Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place names, Volume 28, Osaka)|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本古墳大辞典 (Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun)|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989}} == revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Maezuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maezuka Kofun (Takatsuki), zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the tumulus |Group = |location = Okamoto-chō 33 (Azatsuka-no-Mae), {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 51|Latitude seconds = 8.71 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 35|Longitude seconds = 33.35 |ISO = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |Dimensions = Tumulus length: 94 m |Construction = 5th century |Burial Facility = Unknown (contains a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} made of tuff) |Buried Individual = Unknown |Artifacts = Grave goods, haniwa fragments |Historic Site = None |Cultural Property = Sarcophagus (Osaka Prefecture-designated cultural property) |Notes = |Map = Japan Osaka |Label = Maezuka Kofun |Label position = top }} '''Maezuka Kofun''' (前塚古墳) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The Maezuka Kofun was constructed at the southern end of the Bentenyama Hills in northern Takatsuki City, Osaka Prefecture. Over time, the mound has been partially leveled due to agricultural activities, and the surrounding moat has been interrupted by the construction of a road on the south side. Archaeological investigations have uncovered artifacts and remains of the tumulus, including a sarcophagus unearthed during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a short front section (a defining feature of this type of kofun) oriented westward. Fragments of haniwa, including cylindrical, lid-shaped, and house-shaped examples, have been found on the surface of the mound. The surrounding moat is 10–17 meters wide, and the total length of the kofun, including the moat, is estimated to be 120 meters. Although the specific structure of the burial facility is unclear, it is known that a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} made of tuff was found inside, along with grave goods. Based on the haniwa fragments and sarcophagus design, the Maezuka Kofun is estimated to have been built during the 5th century, placing it in the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Previously, it was believed to be an {{ill|Baizuka|lt=accompanying mound|ja|陪塚}} of the nearby {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}}, but current research classifies it as an earlier kofun. The sarcophagus was designated as an Osaka Prefecture-designated tangible cultural property in 1974. == Dimensions == The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length (including moat): 120 meters * Tumulus length: 94 meters * Rear section (circular): ** Diameter: 69 meters * Front section (rectangular): ** Length: 25 meters ** Width: 25 meters (estimated) The construction plan shows similarities to the nearby {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳}} in Takatsuki City. The surrounding moat mirrors the shape of the tumulus, measuring 16 meters wide on the rear side and 10 meters on the front side. == Burial Facility == [[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun
Designated as an Osaka Prefecture cultural property}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] The burial facility's structure is unknown, but it contained a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}, which was exposed and discovered during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The sarcophagus, made of tuff, measures approximately 2 meters in length, 0.6 meters in width, and 0.8 meters in height. Its lid features a curved shape (so-called "Inrō-lid"), with rope-like protrusions on each side and triangular incisions on the front and back ends. Grave goods such as mirrors, iron swords, and iron halberds were reportedly found, although their details remain unclear. The sarcophagus has been preserved and is designated as an Osaka Prefecture-designated tangible cultural property. The original sarcophagus is displayed at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while a replica is exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校}}. == Cultural Property == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property: ** Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun (archaeological artifact) - Preserved at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property List] (Osaka Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] ({{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, Minamikawachi District) - Preserves and exhibits the original sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校}} ({{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Shinjōchō) - Displays a replica of the sarcophagus. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Local government publications: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=高槻市史 第6巻 考古編 (Takatsuki City History, Volume 6, Archaeological Edition)|year=1973|publisher=Takatsuki City|pages=59–60}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4900 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要9 (Summary of Excavations, No. 9)]|year=1985|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|page=18}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4644 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要13 (Summary of Excavations, No. 13)]|year=1989|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=19–23}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/5175 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要15 (Summary of Excavations, No. 15)]|year=1991|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=20–26}} * Other sources: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本歴史地名大系 28 大阪府の地名 (Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place names, Volume 28, Osaka)|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本古墳大辞典 (Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun)|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Maetsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] City|pages=59–60}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4900 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要9 (Summary of Excavations, No. 9)]|year=1985|publisher=Takatsuki City Based on the haniwa fragments and sarcophagus design, the Maezuka Kofun is estimated to have been built during the 5th century, placing it in the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Previously, it was believed to be an {{ill|Baizuka|lt=accompanying mound|ja|陪塚}} of the nearby {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}}, but current research classifies it as an earlier kofun. The sarcophagus was designated as an Osaka Prefecture-designated tangible cultural property in 1974. == Dimensions == The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length (including moat): 120 meters * Tumulus length: 94 meters * Rear section (circular): ** Diameter: 69 meters * Front section (rectangular): ** Length: 25 meters ** Width: 25 meters (estimated) The construction plan shows similarities to the nearby {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳}} in Takatsuki City. The surrounding moat mirrors the shape of the tumulus, measuring 16 meters wide on the rear side and 10 meters on the front side. == Burial Facility == [[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun
Designated as an Osaka Prefecture cultural property}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] The burial facility's structure is unknown, but it contained a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}, which was exposed and discovered during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The sarcophagus, made of tuff, measures approximately 2 meters in length, 0.6 meters in width, and 0.8 meters in height. Its lid features a curved shape (so-called "Inrō-lid"), with rope-like protrusions on each side and triangular incisions on the front and back ends. Grave goods such as mirrors, iron swords, and iron halberds were reportedly found, although their details remain unclear. The sarcophagus has been preserved and is designated as an Osaka Prefecture-designated tangible cultural property. The original sarcophagus is displayed at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while a replica is exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校}}. == Cultural Property == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property: ** Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun (archaeological artifact) - Preserved at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property List] (Osaka Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] ({{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, Minamikawachi District) - Preserves and exhibits the original sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校}} ({{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Shinjōchō) - Displays a replica of the sarcophagus. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Local government publications: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=高槻市史 第6巻 考古編 (Takatsuki City History, Volume 6, Archaeological Edition)|year=1973|publisher=Takatsuki City|pages=59–60}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4900 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要9 (Summary of Excavations, No. 9)]|year=1985|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|page=18}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4644 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要13 (Summary of Excavations, No. 13)]|year=1989|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=19–23}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/5175 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要15 (Summary of Place names, Volume 28, Osaka)|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本古墳大辞典 (Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun)|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989}} == {{Infobox ancient site |name = Maezuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maezuka Kofun (Takatsuki), zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the tumulus |Group = |location = Okamoto-chō 33 (Azatsuka-no-Mae), {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 51|Latitude seconds = 8.71 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 35|Longitude seconds = 33.35 |ISO = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |Dimensions = Tumulus length: 94 m |Construction = 5th century |Burial Facility = Unknown (contains a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} made of tuff) |Buried Individual = Unknown |Artifacts = Grave goods, haniwa fragments |Historic Site = None |Cultural Property = Sarcophagus (Osaka Prefecture-designated cultural property) |Notes = |Map = Japan Osaka |Label = Maezuka Kofun |Label position = top }} '''Maezuka Kofun''' (前塚古墳) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == The Maezuka Kofun was constructed at the southern end of the Bentenyama Hills in northern Takatsuki City, Osaka Prefecture. Over time, the mound has been partially leveled due to agricultural activities, and the surrounding moat has been interrupted by the construction of a road on the south side. Archaeological investigations have uncovered artifacts and remains of the tumulus, including a sarcophagus unearthed during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a short front section (a defining feature of this type of kofun) oriented westward. Fragments of haniwa, including cylindrical, lid-shaped, and house-shaped examples, have been found on the surface of the mound. The surrounding moat is 10–17 meters wide, and the total length of the kofun, including the moat, is estimated to be 120 meters. Although the specific structure of the burial facility is unclear, it is known that a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} made of tuff was found inside, along with grave goods. Based on the haniwa fragments and sarcophagus design, the Maezuka Kofun is estimated to have been built during the 5th century, placing it in the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Previously, it was believed to be an {{ill|Baizuka|lt=accompanying mound|ja|陪塚}} of the nearby {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}}, but current research classifies it as an earlier kofun. The sarcophagus was designated as an Osaka Prefecture-designated tangible cultural property in 1974. == Dimensions == The dimensions of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length (including moat): 120 meters * Tumulus length: 94 meters * Rear section (circular): ** Diameter: 69 meters * Front section (rectangular): ** Length: 25 meters ** Width: 25 meters (estimated) The construction plan shows similarities to the nearby {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳}} in Takatsuki City. The surrounding moat mirrors the shape of the tumulus, measuring 16 meters wide on the rear side and 10 meters on the front side. == Burial Facility == [[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun
Designated as an Osaka Prefecture cultural property}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] The burial facility's structure is unknown, but it contained a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}, which was exposed and discovered during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The sarcophagus, made of tuff, measures approximately 2 meters in length, 0.6 meters in width, and 0.8 meters in height. Its lid features a curved shape (so-called "Inrō-lid"), with rope-like protrusions on each side and triangular incisions on the front and back ends. Grave goods such as mirrors, iron swords, and iron halberds were reportedly found, although their details remain unclear. The sarcophagus has been preserved and is designated as an Osaka Prefecture-designated tangible cultural property. The original sarcophagus is displayed at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while a replica is exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校}}. == Cultural Property == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property: ** Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun (archaeological artifact) - Preserved at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property List] (Osaka Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] ({{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, Minamikawachi District) - Preserves and exhibits the original sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校}} ({{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Shinjōchō) - Displays a replica of the sarcophagus. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further City|pages=59–60}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4900 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要9 (Summary of Excavations, No. 9)]|year=1985|publisher=Takatsuki City 13)]|year=1989|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=19–23}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/5175 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要15 (Summary of Excavations, No. 15)]|year=1991|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=20–26}} * Other sources: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本歴史地名大系 28 大阪府の地名 (Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place names, Volume 28, Osaka)|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本古墳大辞典 (Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun)|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989}} == Excavations, No. 15)]|year=1991|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=20–26}} * Other sources: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本歴史地名大系 28 大阪府の地名 (Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place names, Volume 28, Osaka)|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本古墳大辞典 (Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun)|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989}} ==
