revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |name = Maezuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maezuka Kofun (Takatsuki), zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the tumulus | part_of = |location = Okamoto-chō 33 (Azatsuka-no-Mae), {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 51|coordinates = 8.71 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 35|coordinates = 33.35 |omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |area = Kofun length: 94 m |Construction = 5th century |burial = Unknown (contains a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} made of tuff) |Buried Individual = Unknown |burial goods = Grave goods, haniwa fragments |other_designation = None |Cultural Property = Sarcophagus (Osaka Prefecture-other_designation1 cultural property) |notes = |map = Japan Osaka |map_dot_label = Maezuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Maezuka Kofun''' (前塚古墳) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is not other_designation1 as a historic site. == Overview == The Maezuka Kofun was constructed at the southern end of the Bentenyama Hills in northern Takatsuki City, Osaka Prefecture. Over time, the mound has been partially leveled due to agricultural activities, and the surrounding moat has been interrupted by the construction of a road on the south side. Archaeological investigations have uncovered burial goods and remains of the tumulus, including a sarcophagus unearthed during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a short front section (a defining feature of this type of kofun) oriented westward. Fragments of haniwa, including cylindrical, lid-shaped, and house-shaped examples, have been found on the surface of the mound. The surrounding moat is 10–17 meters wide, and the total length of the kofun, including the moat, is estimated to be 120 meters. Although the specific structure of the burial facility is unclear, it is known that a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} made of tuff was found inside, along with grave goods. Based on the haniwa fragments and sarcophagus design, the Maezuka Kofun is estimated to have been built during the 5th century, placing it in the middleof the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Previously, it was believed to be an {{ill|Baizuka|lt=accompanying mound|ja|陪塚}} of the nearby {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}}, but current research classifies it as an earlier kofun. The sarcophagus was other_designation1 as an Osaka Prefecture-other_designation1 tangible cultural property in 1974. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length (including moat): 120 meters * Kofun length: 94 meters * Rear section (circular): ** Diameter: 69 meters * Front section (rectangular): ** Length: 25 meters ** Width: 25 meters (estimated) The construction plan shows similarities to the nearby {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳}} in Takatsuki City. The surrounding moat mirrors the type of the tumulus, measuring 16 meters wide on the rear side and 10 meters on the front side. == burial == [[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun
Designated as an Osaka Prefecture cultural property}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] The burial facility's structure is unknown, but it contained a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}, which was exposed and discovered during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The sarcophagus, made of tuff, measures approximately 2 meters in length, 0.6 meters in width, and 0.8 meters in height. Its lid features a curved type (so-called "Inrō-lid"), with rope-like protrusions on each side and triangular incisions on the front and back ends. Grave goods such as mirrors, iron swords, and iron halberds were reportedly found, although their details remain unclear. The sarcophagus has been preserved and is other_designation1 as an Osaka Prefecture-other_designation1 tangible cultural property. The original sarcophagus is displayed at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while a replica is exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校}}. == Cultural Property == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property: ** Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun (archaeological artifact) - Preserved at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property List] (Osaka Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] ({{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, Minamikawachi District) - Preserves and exhibits the original sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校}} ({{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Shinjōchō) - Displays a replica of the sarcophagus. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Local government publications: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=高槻市史 第6巻 考古編 (Takatsuki City History, Volume 6, Archaeological Edition)|year=1973|publisher=Takatsuki City|pages=59–60}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4900 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要9 (Summary of Excavations, No. 9)]|year=1985|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|page=18}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4644 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要13 (Summary of Excavations, No. 13)]|year=1989|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=19–23}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/5175 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要15 (Summary of Excavations, No. 15)]|year=1991|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=20–26}} * Other sources: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本歴史地名大系 28 大阪府の地名 (Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place names, Volume 28, Osaka)|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本古墳大辞典 (Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun)|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[sn:Maetsuka Kofun]][[Category:dd]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Maetsuka Kofun}}[[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[sn:Maetsuka Based on the haniwa fragments and sarcophagus design, the Maezuka Kofun is estimated to have been built during the 5th century, placing it in the middleof the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Previously, it was believed to be an {{ill|Baizuka|lt=accompanying mound|ja|陪塚}} of the nearby {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}}, but current research classifies it as an earlier kofun. The sarcophagus was other_designation1 as an Osaka Prefecture-other_designation1 tangible cultural property in 1974. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length (including moat): 120 meters * Kofun length: 94 meters * Rear section (circular): ** Diameter: 69 meters * Front section (rectangular): ** Length: 25 meters ** Width: 25 meters (estimated) The construction plan shows similarities to the nearby {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳}} in Takatsuki City. The surrounding moat mirrors the type of the tumulus, measuring 16 meters wide on the rear side and 10 meters on the front side. == burial == [[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun
Designated as an Osaka Prefecture cultural property}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] The burial facility's structure is unknown, but it contained a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}, which was exposed and discovered during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The sarcophagus, made of tuff, measures approximately 2 meters in length, 0.6 meters in width, and 0.8 meters in height. Its lid features a curved type (so-called "Inrō-lid"), with rope-like protrusions on each side and triangular incisions on the front and back ends. Grave goods such as mirrors, iron swords, and iron halberds were reportedly found, although their details remain unclear. The sarcophagus has been preserved and is other_designation1 as an Osaka Prefecture-other_designation1 tangible cultural property. The original sarcophagus is displayed at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while a replica is exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校}}. == Cultural Property == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property: ** Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun (archaeological artifact) - Preserved at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property List] (Osaka Prefecture website). == Related Full view of the tumulus | part_of = |location = Okamoto-chō 33 (Azatsuka-no-Mae), {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 51|coordinates = 8.71 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 35|coordinates = 33.35 |omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |area = Kofun length: 94 m |Construction = 5th century |burial = Unknown (contains a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} made of tuff) |Interred_individual = Unknown |burial goods = Grave goods, haniwa fragments |other_designation = None |Cultural Property = Sarcophagus (Osaka Prefecture-other_designation1 cultural property) |notes = |map = Japan Osaka |map_dot_label = Maezuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Maezuka Kofun''' (前塚古墳) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is not other_designation1 as a historic site. == Overview == The Maezuka Kofun was constructed at the southern end of the Bentenyama Hills in northern Takatsuki City, Osaka Prefecture. Over time, the mound has been partially leveled due to agricultural activities, and the surrounding moat has been interrupted by the construction of a road on the south side. Archaeological investigations have uncovered burial goods and remains of the tumulus, including a sarcophagus unearthed during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a short front section (a defining feature of this type of kofun) oriented westward. Fragments of haniwa, including cylindrical, lid-shaped, and house-shaped examples, have been found on the surface of the mound. The surrounding moat is 10–17 meters wide, and the total length of the kofun, including the moat, is estimated to be 120 meters. Although the specific structure of the burial facility is unclear, it is known that a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} made of tuff was found inside, along with grave goods. Based on the haniwa fragments and sarcophagus design, the Maezuka Kofun is estimated to have been built during the 5th century, placing it in the middleof the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Previously, it was believed to be an {{ill|Baizuka|lt=accompanying mound|ja|陪塚}} of the nearby {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}}, but current research classifies it as an earlier kofun. The sarcophagus was other_designation1 as an Osaka Prefecture-other_designation1 tangible cultural property in 1974. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length (including moat): 120 meters * Kofun length: 94 meters * Rear section (circular): ** Diameter: 69 meters * Front section (rectangular): ** Length: 25 meters ** Width: 25 meters (estimated) The construction plan shows similarities to the nearby {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳}} in Takatsuki City. The surrounding moat mirrors the type of the tumulus, measuring 16 meters wide on the rear side and 10 meters on the front side. == burial == [[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun
Designated as an Osaka Prefecture cultural property}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] The burial facility's structure is unknown, but it contained a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}, which was exposed and discovered during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The sarcophagus, made of tuff, measures approximately 2 meters in length, 0.6 meters in width, and 0.8 meters in height. Its lid features a curved type (so-called "Inrō-lid"), with rope-like protrusions on each side and triangular incisions on the front and back ends. Grave goods such as mirrors, iron swords, and iron halberds were reportedly found, although their details remain unclear. The sarcophagus has been preserved and is other_designation1 as an Osaka Prefecture-other_designation1 tangible cultural property. The original sarcophagus is displayed at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while a replica is exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校}}. == Cultural Property == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property: ** Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun (archaeological artifact) - Preserved at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property List] (Osaka Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] ({{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, Minamikawachi District) - Preserves and exhibits the original sarcophagus from The tumulus is not other_designation1 as a historic site. == Overview == The Maezuka Kofun was constructed at the southern end of the Bentenyama Hills in northern Takatsuki City, Osaka Prefecture. Over time, the mound has been partially leveled due to agricultural activities, and the surrounding moat has been interrupted by the construction of a road on the south side. Archaeological investigations have uncovered burial goods and remains of the tumulus, including a sarcophagus unearthed during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a short front section (a defining feature of this type of kofun) oriented westward. Fragments of haniwa, including cylindrical, lid-shaped, and house-shaped examples, have been found on the surface of the mound. The surrounding moat is 10–17 meters wide, and the total length of the kofun, including the moat, is estimated to be 120 meters. Although the specific structure of the burial facility is unclear, it is known that a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} made of tuff was found inside, along with grave goods. Based on the haniwa fragments and sarcophagus design, the Maezuka Kofun is estimated to have been built during the 5th century, placing it in the middleof the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Previously, it was believed to be an {{ill|Baizuka|lt=accompanying mound|ja|陪塚}} of the nearby {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}}, but current research classifies it as an earlier kofun. The sarcophagus was other_designation1 as an Osaka Prefecture-other_designation1 tangible cultural property in 1974. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length (including moat): 120 meters * Kofun length: 94 meters * Rear section (circular): ** Diameter: 69 meters * Front section (rectangular): ** Length: 25 meters ** Width: 25 meters (estimated) The construction plan shows similarities to the nearby {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳}} in Takatsuki City. The surrounding moat mirrors the type of the tumulus, measuring 16 meters wide on the rear side and 10 meters on the front side. == burial == [[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun
Designated as an Osaka Prefecture cultural property}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] The burial facility's structure is unknown, but it contained a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}, which was exposed and discovered during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The sarcophagus, made of tuff, measures approximately 2 meters in length, 0.6 meters in width, and 0.8 meters in height. Its lid features a curved type (so-called "Inrō-lid"), with rope-like protrusions on each side and triangular incisions on the front and back ends. Grave goods such as mirrors, iron swords, and iron halberds were reportedly found, although their details remain unclear. The sarcophagus has been preserved and is other_designation1 as an Osaka Prefecture-other_designation1 tangible cultural property. The original sarcophagus is displayed at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while a replica is exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校}}. == Cultural Property == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property: ** Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun (archaeological artifact) - Preserved at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property List] (Osaka Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] ({{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, Minamikawachi District) - Preserves and exhibits the original sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校}} ({{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Shinjōchō) - Displays a replica of the sarcophagus. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further City|pages=59–60}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4900 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要9 (Summary of Excavations, No. 9)]|year=1985|publisher=Takatsuki City Excavations, No. 15)]|year=1991|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=20–26}} * Other sources: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本歴史地名大系 28 大阪府の地名 (Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place names, Volume 28, Osaka)|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本古墳大辞典 (Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun)|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989}} == Full view of the tumulus | part_of = |location = Okamoto-chō 33 (Azatsuka-no-Mae), {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 51|coordinates = 8.71 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 35|coordinates = 33.35 |omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |area = Kofun length: 94 m |Construction = 5th century |burial = Unknown (contains a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} made of tuff) |Interred_individual = Unknown |burial goods = Grave goods, haniwa fragments |other_designation = None |Cultural Property = Sarcophagus (Osaka Prefecture-other_designation1 cultural property) |notes = |map = Japan Osaka |map_dot_label = Maezuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Maezuka Kofun''' (前塚古墳) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is not other_designation1 as a historic site. == Overview == The Maezuka Kofun was constructed at the southern end of the Bentenyama Hills in northern Takatsuki City, Osaka Prefecture. Over time, the mound has been partially leveled due to agricultural activities, and the surrounding moat has been interrupted by the construction of a road on the south side. Archaeological investigations have uncovered burial goods and remains of the tumulus, including a sarcophagus unearthed during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a short front section (a defining feature of this type of kofun) oriented westward. Fragments of haniwa, including cylindrical, lid-shaped, and house-shaped examples, have been found on the surface of the mound. The surrounding moat is 10–17 meters wide, and the total length of the kofun, including the moat, is estimated to be 120 meters. Although the specific structure of the burial facility is unclear, it is known that a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} made of tuff was found inside, along with grave goods. Based on the haniwa fragments and sarcophagus design, the Maezuka Kofun is estimated to have been built during the 5th century, placing it in the middleof the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Previously, it was believed to be an {{ill|Baizuka|lt=accompanying mound|ja|陪塚}} of the nearby {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}}, but current research classifies it as an earlier kofun. The sarcophagus was other_designation1 as an Osaka Prefecture-other_designation1 tangible cultural property in 1974. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length (including moat): 120 meters * Kofun length: 94 meters * Rear section (circular): ** Diameter: 69 meters * Front section (rectangular): ** Length: 25 meters ** Width: 25 meters (estimated) The construction plan shows similarities to the nearby {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳}} in Takatsuki City. The surrounding moat mirrors the type of the tumulus, measuring 16 meters wide on the rear side and 10 meters on the front side. == burial == [[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun
Designated as an Osaka Prefecture cultural property}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] The burial facility's structure is unknown, but it contained a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}, which was exposed and discovered during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The sarcophagus, made of tuff, measures approximately 2 meters in length, 0.6 meters in width, and 0.8 meters in height. Its lid features a curved type (so-called "Inrō-lid"), with rope-like protrusions on each side and triangular incisions on the front and back ends. Grave goods such as mirrors, iron swords, and iron halberds were reportedly found, although their details remain unclear. The sarcophagus has been preserved and is other_designation1 as an Osaka Prefecture-other_designation1 tangible cultural property. The original sarcophagus is displayed at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while a replica is exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校}}. == Cultural Property == === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property === * Tangible Cultural Property: ** Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun (archaeological artifact) - Preserved at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Property List] (Osaka Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] ({{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, Minamikawachi District) - Preserves and exhibits the original sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校}} ({{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Shinjōchō) - Displays a replica of the sarcophagus. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Full view of the tumulus | part_of = |location = Okamoto-chō 33 (Azatsuka-no-Mae), {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 51|coordinates = 8.71 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 35|coordinates = 33.35 |omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |area = Kofun length: 94 m |Construction = 5th century |burial = Unknown (contains a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} made of tuff) |Interred_individual = Unknown |burial goods = Grave goods, haniwa fragments |other_designation = None |designation1 = Sarcophagus (Osaka Prefecture-other_designation1 cultural property) |notes = |map = Japan Osaka |map_dot_label = Maezuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Maezuka Kofun''' (前塚古墳) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is not other_designation1 as a historic site. == Overview == The Maezuka Kofun was constructed at the southern end of the Bentenyama Hills in northern Takatsuki City, Osaka Prefecture. Over time, the mound has been partially leveled due to agricultural activities, and the surrounding moat has been interrupted by the construction of a road on the south side. Archaeological investigations have uncovered burial goods and remains of the tumulus, including a sarcophagus unearthed during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a short front section (a defining feature of this type of kofun) oriented westward. Fragments of haniwa, including cylindrical, lid-shaped, and house-shaped examples, have been found on the surface of the mound. The surrounding moat is 10–17 meters wide, and the total length of the kofun, including the moat, is estimated to be 120 meters. Although the specific structure of the burial facility is unclear, it is known that a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} made of tuff was found inside, along with grave goods. Based on the haniwa fragments and sarcophagus design, the Maezuka Kofun is estimated to have been built during the 5th century, placing it in the middleof the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Previously, it was believed to be an {{ill|Baizuka|lt=accompanying mound|ja|陪塚}} of the nearby {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}}, but current research classifies it as an earlier kofun. The sarcophagus was other_designation1 as an Osaka Prefecture-other_designation1 tangible cultural property in 1974. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length (including moat): 120 meters * Kofun length: 94 meters * Rear section (circular): ** Diameter: 69 meters * Front section (rectangular): ** Length: 25 meters ** Width: 25 meters (estimated) The construction plan shows similarities to the nearby {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳}} in Takatsuki City. The surrounding moat mirrors the type of the tumulus, measuring 16 meters wide on the rear side and 10 meters on the front side. == burial == [[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun
Designated as an Osaka Prefecture cultural property}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] The burial facility's structure is unknown, but it contained a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}, which was exposed and discovered during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The sarcophagus, made of tuff, measures approximately 2 meters in length, 0.6 meters in width, and 0.8 meters in height. Its lid features a curved type (so-called "Inrō-lid"), with rope-like protrusions on each side and triangular incisions on the front and back ends. Grave goods such as mirrors, iron swords, and iron halberds were reportedly found, although their details remain unclear. The sarcophagus has been preserved and is other_designation1 as an Osaka Prefecture-other_designation1 tangible cultural property. The original sarcophagus is displayed at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while a replica is exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校}}. == designation1 == === Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 === * Tangible designation1: ** Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun (archaeological artifact) - Preserved at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 List] (Osaka Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] ({{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, Minamikawachi District) - Preserves and exhibits the original sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校}} ({{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Shinjōchō) - Displays a replica of the sarcophagus. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |name = Maezuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maezuka Kofun (Takatsuki), zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the tumulus | part_of = |location = Okamoto-chō 33 (Azatsuka-no-Mae), {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 51|coordinates = 8.71 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 35|coordinates = 33.35 |omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |area = Kofun length: 94 m |Construction = 5th century |burial = Unknown (contains a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} made of tuff) |Interred_individual = Unknown |burial goods = Grave goods, haniwa fragments |other_designation = None |designation1 = Sarcophagus (Osaka Prefecture-other_designation1 cultural property) |notes = |map = Japan Osaka |map_dot_label = Maezuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Maezuka Kofun''' (前塚古墳) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is not other_designation1 as a historic site. == Overview == The Maezuka Kofun was constructed at the southern end of the Bentenyama Hills in northern Takatsuki City, Osaka Prefecture. Over time, the mound has been partially leveled due to agricultural activities, and the surrounding moat has been interrupted by the construction of a road on the south side. Archaeological investigations have uncovered burial goods and remains of the tumulus, including a sarcophagus unearthed during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a short front section (a defining feature of this type of kofun) oriented westward. Fragments of haniwa, including cylindrical, lid-shaped, and house-shaped examples, have been found on the surface of the mound. The surrounding moat is 10–17 meters wide, and the total length of the kofun, including the moat, is estimated to be 120 meters. Although the specific structure of the burial facility is unclear, it is known that a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} made of tuff was found inside, along with grave goods. Based on the haniwa fragments and sarcophagus design, the Maezuka Kofun is estimated to have been built during the 5th century, placing it in the middleof the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Previously, it was believed to be an {{ill|Baizuka|lt=accompanying mound|ja|陪塚}} of the nearby {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}}, but current research classifies it as an earlier kofun. The sarcophagus was other_designation1 as an Osaka Prefecture-other_designation1 tangible cultural property in 1974. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length (including moat): 120 meters * Kofun length: 94 meters * Rear section (circular): ** Diameter: 69 meters * Front section (rectangular): ** Length: 25 meters ** Width: 25 meters (estimated) The construction plan shows similarities to the nearby {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳}} in Takatsuki City. The surrounding moat mirrors the type of the tumulus, measuring 16 meters wide on the rear side and 10 meters on the front side. == burial == [[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun
Designated as an Osaka Prefecture cultural property}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] The burial facility's structure is unknown, but it contained a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}, which was exposed and discovered during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The sarcophagus, made of tuff, measures approximately 2 meters in length, 0.6 meters in width, and 0.8 meters in height. Its lid features a curved type (so-called "Inrō-lid"), with rope-like protrusions on each side and triangular incisions on the front and back ends. Grave goods such as mirrors, iron swords, and iron halberds were reportedly found, although their details remain unclear. The sarcophagus has been preserved and is other_designation1 as an Osaka Prefecture-other_designation1 tangible cultural property. The original sarcophagus is displayed at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while a replica is exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校}}. == designation1 == === Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 === * Tangible designation1: ** Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun (archaeological artifact) - Preserved at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 List] (Osaka Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] ({{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, Minamikawachi District) - Preserves and exhibits the original sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校}} ({{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Shinjōchō) - Displays a replica of the sarcophagus. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Local government publications: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=高槻市史 第6巻 考古編 (Takatsuki City History, Volume 6, Archaeological Edition)|year=1973|publisher=Takatsuki City|pages=59–60}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4900 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要9 (Summary of Excavations, No. 9)]|year=1985|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|page=18}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4644 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要13 (Summary of Excavations, No. 13)]|year=1989|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=19–23}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/5175 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要15 (Summary of Excavations, No. 15)]|year=1991|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=20–26}} * Other sources: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本歴史地名大系 28 大阪府の地名 (Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place names, Volume 28, Osaka)|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本古墳大辞典 (Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun)|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989}} == Related Articles == * Previously, it was believed to be an {{ill|Baizuka|lt=accompanying mound|ja|陪塚}} of the nearby {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}}, but current research classifies it as an earlier kofun. The sarcophagus was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property in 1974. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length (including moat): 120 meters * Kofun length: 94 meters * Rear section (circular): ** Diameter: 69 meters * Front section (rectangular): ** Length: 25 meters ** Width: 25 meters (estimated) The construction plan shows similarities to the nearby {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳}} in Takatsuki City. The surrounding moat mirrors the type of the tumulus, measuring 16 meters wide on the rear side and 10 meters on the front side. == burial == [[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun
Designated as an Osaka Prefecture cultural property}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] The burial facility's structure is unknown, but it contained a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}, which was exposed and discovered during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. Full view of the tumulus | part_of = |location = Okamoto-chō 33 (Azatsuka-no-Mae), {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 51|coordinates = 8.71 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 35|coordinates = 33.35 |omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |area = Kofun length: 94 m |Construction = 5th century |burial = Unknown (contains a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} made of tuff) |Interred_individual = Unknown |burial goods = Grave goods, haniwa fragments |Historic Site = None |designation1 = Sarcophagus (Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 cultural property) |notes = |map = Japan Osaka |map_dot_label = Maezuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Maezuka Kofun''' (前塚古墳) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == The Maezuka Kofun was constructed at the southern end of the Bentenyama Hills in northern Takatsuki City, Osaka Prefecture. Over time, the mound has been partially leveled due to agricultural activities, and the surrounding moat has been interrupted by the construction of a road on the south side. Archaeological investigations have uncovered burial goods and remains of the tumulus, including a sarcophagus unearthed during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a short front section (a defining feature of this type of kofun) oriented westward. Fragments of haniwa, including cylindrical, lid-shaped, and house-shaped examples, have been found on the surface of the mound. The surrounding moat is 10–17 meters wide, and the total length of the kofun, including the moat, is estimated to be 120 meters. Although the specific structure of the burial facility is unclear, it is known that a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} made of tuff was found inside, along with grave goods. Based on the haniwa fragments and sarcophagus design, the Maezuka Kofun is estimated to have been built during the 5th century, placing it in the middleof the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Previously, it was believed to be an {{ill|Baizuka|lt=accompanying mound|ja|陪塚}} of the nearby {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}}, but current research classifies it as an earlier kofun. The sarcophagus was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property in 1974. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length (including moat): 120 meters * Kofun length: 94 meters * Rear section (circular): ** Diameter: 69 meters * Front section (rectangular): ** Length: 25 meters ** Width: 25 meters (estimated) The construction plan shows similarities to the nearby {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳}} in Takatsuki City. The surrounding moat mirrors the type of the tumulus, measuring 16 meters wide on the rear side and 10 meters on the front side. == burial == [[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun
Designated as an Osaka Prefecture cultural property}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] The burial facility's structure is unknown, but it contained a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}, which was exposed and discovered during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The sarcophagus, made of tuff, measures approximately 2 meters in length, 0.6 meters in width, and 0.8 meters in height. Its lid features a curved type (so-called "Inrō-lid"), with rope-like protrusions on each side and triangular incisions on the front and back ends. Grave goods such as mirrors, iron swords, and iron halberds were reportedly found, although their details remain unclear. The sarcophagus has been preserved and is Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property. The original sarcophagus is displayed at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while a replica is exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校}}. == designation1 == === Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 === * Tangible designation1: ** Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun (archaeological artifact) - Preserved at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 List] (Osaka Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] ({{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, Minamikawachi District) - Preserves and exhibits the original sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校}} ({{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Shinjōchō) - Displays a replica of the sarcophagus. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4644 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要13 (Summary of Excavations, No. 13)]|year=1989|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} |name = Maezuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maezuka Kofun (Takatsuki), zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the tumulus | part_of = |location = Okamoto-chō 33 (Azatsuka-no-Mae), {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 51|coordinates = 8.71 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 35|coordinates = 33.35 |omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |area = Kofun length: 94 m |Construction = 5th century |burial = Unknown (contains a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} made of tuff) |Interred_individual = Unknown |burial goods = Grave goods, haniwa fragments |Historic Site = None |designation1 = Sarcophagus (Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 cultural property) |notes = |map = Japan Osaka |map_dot_label = Maezuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Maezuka Kofun''' (前塚古墳) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == The Maezuka Kofun was constructed at the southern end of the Bentenyama Hills in northern Takatsuki City, Osaka Prefecture. Over time, the mound has been partially leveled due to agricultural activities, and the surrounding moat has been interrupted by the construction of a road on the south side. Archaeological investigations have uncovered burial goods and remains of the tumulus, including a sarcophagus unearthed during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a short front section (a defining feature of this type of kofun) oriented westward. Fragments of haniwa, including cylindrical, lid-shaped, and house-shaped examples, have been found on the surface of the mound. The surrounding moat is 10–17 meters wide, and the total length of the kofun, including the moat, is estimated to be 120 meters. Although the specific structure of the burial facility is unclear, it is known that a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} made of tuff was found inside, along with grave goods. Based on the haniwa fragments and sarcophagus design, the Maezuka Kofun is estimated to have been built during the 5th century, placing it in the middleof the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Previously, it was believed to be an {{ill|Baizuka|lt=accompanying mound|ja|陪塚}} of the nearby {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}}, but current research classifies it as an earlier kofun. The sarcophagus was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property in 1974. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length (including moat): 120 meters * Kofun length: 94 meters * Rear section (circular): ** Diameter: 69 meters * Front section (rectangular): ** Length: 25 meters ** Width: 25 meters (estimated) The construction plan shows similarities to the nearby {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳}} in Takatsuki City. The surrounding moat mirrors the type of the tumulus, measuring 16 meters wide on the rear side and 10 meters on the front side. == burial == [[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun
Designated as an Osaka Prefecture cultural property}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] The burial facility's structure is unknown, but it contained a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}, which was exposed and discovered during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The sarcophagus, made of tuff, measures approximately 2 meters in length, 0.6 meters in width, and 0.8 meters in height. Its lid features a curved type (so-called "Inrō-lid"), with rope-like protrusions on each side and triangular incisions on the front and back ends. Grave goods such as mirrors, iron swords, and iron halberds were reportedly found, although their details remain unclear. The sarcophagus has been preserved and is Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property. The original sarcophagus is displayed at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while a replica is exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校}}. == designation1 == === Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 === * Tangible designation1: ** Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun (archaeological artifact) - Preserved at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 List] (Osaka Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] ({{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, Minamikawachi District) - Preserves and exhibits the original sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校}} ({{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Shinjōchō) - Displays a replica of the sarcophagus. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Local government publications: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=高槻市史 第6巻 考古編 (Takatsuki City History, Volume 6, Archaeological Edition)|year=1973|publisher=Takatsuki City|pages=59–60}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4900 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要9 (Summary of Excavations, No. 9)]|year=1985|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|page=18}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4644 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要13 (Summary of Excavations, No. 13)]|year=1989|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=19–23}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/5175 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要15 (Summary of Excavations, No. 15)]|year=1991|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=20–26}} * Other sources: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本歴史地名大系 28 大阪府の地名 (Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place names, Volume 28, Osaka)|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本古墳大辞典 (Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun)|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989}} == Related Articles == * revision.comment: Bot: Replaced ill templates with appended |12=draft and created corresponding redirect pages.
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=draft}} |name = Maezuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maezuka Kofun (Takatsuki), zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the tumulus | part_of = |location = Okamoto-chō 33 (Azatsuka-no-Mae), {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 51|coordinates = 8.71 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 35|coordinates = 33.35 |omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} |area = Kofun length: 94 m |Construction = 5th century |burial = Unknown (contains a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}} made of tuff) |Interred_individual = Unknown |burial goods = Grave goods, haniwa fragments |Historic Site = None |designation1 = Sarcophagus (Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 cultural property) |notes = |map = Japan Osaka |map_dot_label = Maezuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Maezuka Kofun''' (前塚古墳) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == The Maezuka Kofun was constructed at the southern end of the Bentenyama Hills in northern Takatsuki City, Osaka Prefecture. Over time, the mound has been partially leveled due to agricultural activities, and the surrounding moat has been interrupted by the construction of a road on the south side. Archaeological investigations have uncovered burial goods and remains of the tumulus, including a sarcophagus unearthed during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a short front section (a defining feature of this type of kofun) oriented westward. Fragments of haniwa, including cylindrical, lid-shaped, and house-shaped examples, have been found on the surface of the mound. The surrounding moat is 10–17 meters wide, and the total length of the kofun, including the moat, is estimated to be 120 meters. Although the specific structure of the burial facility is unclear, it is known that a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}} made of tuff was found inside, along with grave goods. Based on the haniwa fragments and sarcophagus design, the Maezuka Kofun is estimated to have been built during the 5th century, placing it in the middleof the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. Previously, it was believed to be an {{ill|Baizuka|lt=accompanying mound|ja|陪塚|12=draft}} of the nearby {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|12=draft}}, but current research classifies it as an earlier kofun. The sarcophagus was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property in 1974. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length (including moat): 120 meters * Kofun length: 94 meters * Rear section (circular): ** Diameter: 69 meters * Front section (rectangular): ** Length: 25 meters ** Width: 25 meters (estimated) The construction plan shows similarities to the nearby {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳|12=draft}} in Takatsuki City. The surrounding moat mirrors the type of the tumulus, measuring 16 meters wide on the rear side and 10 meters on the front side. == burial == [[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun
Designated as an Osaka Prefecture cultural property}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] The burial facility's structure is unknown, but it contained a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}}, which was exposed and discovered during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The sarcophagus, made of tuff, measures approximately 2 meters in length, 0.6 meters in width, and 0.8 meters in height. Its lid features a curved type (so-called "Inrō-lid"), with rope-like protrusions on each side and triangular incisions on the front and back ends. Grave goods such as mirrors, iron swords, and iron halberds were reportedly found, although their details remain unclear. The sarcophagus has been preserved and is Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property. The original sarcophagus is displayed at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while a replica is exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校|12=draft}}. == designation1 == === Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 === * Tangible designation1: ** Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun (archaeological artifact) - Preserved at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 List] (Osaka Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] ({{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町|12=draft}}, Minamikawachi District) - Preserves and exhibits the original sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校|12=draft}} ({{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市|12=draft}}, Shinjōchō) - Displays a replica of the sarcophagus. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Local government publications: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=高槻市史 第6巻 考古編 (Takatsuki City History, Volume 6, Archaeological Edition)|year=1973|publisher=Takatsuki City|pages=59–60}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4900 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要9 (Summary of Excavations, No. 9)]|year=1985|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|page=18}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4644 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要13 (Summary of Excavations, No. 13)]|year=1989|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=19–23}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/5175 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要15 (Summary of Excavations, No. 15)]|year=1991|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=20–26}} * Other sources: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本歴史地名大系 28 大阪府の地名 (Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place names, Volume 28, Osaka)|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本古墳大辞典 (Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun)|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳|12=draft}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Maetsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takatsuki]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11-1月4日 (旧暦)-dd]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Maetsuka Kofun]] [[ja:前塚古墳 (高槻市)]] [[ja:前塚古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Maetsuka Kofun}}

The surrounding moat mirrors the type of the tumulus, measuring 16 meters wide on the rear side and 10 meters on the front side. == burial == [[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun
Designated as an Osaka Prefecture cultural property}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] The burial facility's structure is unknown, but it contained a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}}, which was exposed and discovered during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The sarcophagus, made of tuff, measures approximately 2 meters in length, 0.6 meters in width, and 0.8 meters in height. Its lid features a curved type (so-called "Inrō-lid"), with rope-like protrusions on each side and triangular incisions on the front and back ends. Grave goods such as mirrors, iron swords, and iron halberds were reportedly found, although their details remain unclear. The sarcophagus has been preserved and is Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property. The original sarcophagus is displayed at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while a replica is exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校|12=draft}}. == designation1 == === Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 === * Tangible designation1: ** Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun (archaeological artifact) - Preserved at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 List] (Osaka Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] ({{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町|12=draft}}, Minamikawachi District) - Preserves and exhibits the original sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校|12=draft}} ({{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市|12=draft}}, Shinjōchō) - Displays a replica of the sarcophagus. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Local government publications: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=高槻市史 第6巻 考古編 (Takatsuki City History, Volume 6, Archaeological Edition)|year=1973|publisher=Takatsuki City|pages=59–60}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4900 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要9 (Summary of Excavations, No. 9)]|year=1985|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|page=18}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4644 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要13 (Summary of Excavations, No. Excavations, No. 15)]|year=1991|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=20–26}} * Other sources: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本歴史地名大系 28 大阪府の地名 (Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place names, Volume 28, Osaka)|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本古墳大辞典 (Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun)|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989}} == Full view of the tumulus | part_of = |location = Okamoto-chō 33 (Azatsuka-no-Mae), {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 51|coordinates = 8.71 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 35|coordinates = 33.35 |omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} |area = Kofun length: 94 m |Construction = 5th century |burial = Unknown (contains a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}} made of tuff) |Interred_individual = Unknown |burial goods = Grave goods, haniwa fragments |Historic Site = None |designation1 = Sarcophagus (Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 cultural property) |notes = |map = Japan Osaka |map_dot_label = Maezuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Maezuka Kofun''' (前塚古墳) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == The Maezuka Kofun was constructed at the southern end of the Bentenyama Hills in northern Takatsuki City, Osaka Prefecture. Over time, the mound has been partially leveled due to agricultural activities, and the surrounding moat has been interrupted by the construction of a road on the south side. Archaeological investigations have uncovered burial goods and remains of the tumulus, including a sarcophagus unearthed during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a short front section (a defining feature of this type of kofun) oriented westward. Fragments of haniwa, including cylindrical, lid-shaped, and house-shaped examples, have been found on the surface of the mound. The surrounding moat is 10–17 meters wide, and the total length of the kofun, including the moat, is estimated to be 120 meters. Although the specific structure of the burial facility is unclear, it is known that a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}} made of tuff was found inside, along with grave goods. Based on the haniwa fragments and sarcophagus design, the Maezuka Kofun is estimated to have been built during the 5th century, placing it in the middleof the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. Previously, it was believed to be an {{ill|Baizuka|lt=accompanying mound|ja|陪塚|12=draft}} of the nearby {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|12=draft}}, but current research classifies it as an earlier kofun. The sarcophagus was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property in 1974. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length (including moat): 120 meters * Kofun length: 94 meters * Rear section (circular): ** Diameter: 69 meters * Front section (rectangular): ** Length: 25 meters ** Width: 25 meters (estimated) The construction plan shows similarities to the nearby {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳|12=draft}} in Takatsuki City. The surrounding moat mirrors the type of the tumulus, measuring 16 meters wide on the rear side and 10 meters on the front side. == burial == [[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun
Designated as an Osaka Prefecture cultural property}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] The burial facility's structure is unknown, but it contained a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}}, which was exposed and discovered during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The sarcophagus, made of tuff, measures approximately 2 meters in length, 0.6 meters in width, and 0.8 meters in height. Its lid features a curved type (so-called "Inrō-lid"), with rope-like protrusions on each side and triangular incisions on the front and back ends. Grave goods such as mirrors, iron swords, and iron halberds were reportedly found, although their details remain unclear. The sarcophagus has been preserved and is Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property. The original sarcophagus is displayed at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while a replica is exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校|12=draft}}. == designation1 == === Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 === * Tangible designation1: ** Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun (archaeological artifact) - Preserved at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 List] (Osaka Prefecture website). == Related Facilities Full view of the tumulus | part_of = |location = Okamoto-chō 33 (Azatsuka-no-Mae), {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 51|coordinates = 8.71 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 35|coordinates = 33.35 |omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} |area = Kofun length: 94 m |Construction = 5th century |burial = Unknown (contains a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}} made of tuff) |Interred_individual = Unknown |burial goods = Grave goods, haniwa fragments |Historic Site = None |designation1 = Sarcophagus (Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 cultural property) |notes = |map = Japan Osaka |map_dot_label = Maezuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Maezuka Kofun''' (前塚古墳) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == The Maezuka Kofun was constructed at the southern end of the Bentenyama Hills in northern Takatsuki City, Osaka Prefecture. Over time, the mound has been partially leveled due to agricultural activities, and the surrounding moat has been interrupted by the construction of a road on the south side. Archaeological investigations have uncovered burial goods and remains of the tumulus, including a sarcophagus unearthed during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a short front section (a defining feature of this type of kofun) oriented westward. Fragments of haniwa, including cylindrical, lid-shaped, and house-shaped examples, have been found on the surface of the mound. The surrounding moat is 10–17 meters wide, and the total length of the kofun, including the moat, is estimated to be 120 meters. Although the specific structure of the burial facility is unclear, it is known that a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}} made of tuff was found inside, along with grave goods. Based on the haniwa fragments and sarcophagus design, the Maezuka Kofun is estimated to have been built during the 5th century, placing it in the middleof the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. Previously, it was believed to be an {{ill|Baizuka|lt=accompanying mound|ja|陪塚|12=draft}} of the nearby {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|12=draft}}, but current research classifies it as an earlier kofun. The sarcophagus was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property in 1974. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length (including moat): 120 meters * Kofun length: 94 meters * Rear section (circular): ** Diameter: 69 meters * Front section (rectangular): ** Length: 25 meters ** Width: 25 meters (estimated) The construction plan shows similarities to the nearby {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳|12=draft}} in Takatsuki City. The surrounding moat mirrors the type of the tumulus, measuring 16 meters wide on the rear side and 10 meters on the front side. == burial == [[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun
Designated as an Osaka Prefecture cultural property}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] The burial facility's structure is unknown, but it contained a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}}, which was exposed and discovered during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The sarcophagus, made of tuff, measures approximately 2 meters in length, 0.6 meters in width, and 0.8 meters in height. Its lid features a curved type (so-called "Inrō-lid"), with rope-like protrusions on each side and triangular incisions on the front and back ends. Grave goods such as mirrors, iron swords, and iron halberds were reportedly found, although their details remain unclear. The sarcophagus has been preserved and is Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property. The original sarcophagus is displayed at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while a replica is exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校|12=draft}}. == designation1 == === Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 === * Tangible designation1: ** Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun (archaeological artifact) - Preserved at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 List] (Osaka Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] ({{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町|12=draft}}, Minamikawachi District) - Preserves and exhibits the original sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校|12=draft}} ({{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市|12=draft}}, Shinjōchō) - Displays a replica of the sarcophagus. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Local government publications: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=高槻市史 第6巻 考古編 (Takatsuki City History, Volume 6, Archaeological Edition)|year=1973|publisher=Takatsuki City|pages=59–60}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4900 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要9 (Summary of Excavations, No. 9)]|year=1985|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|page=18}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4644 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要13 (Summary of Excavations, No. 13)]|year=1989|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=19–23}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/5175 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要15 (Summary of Excavations, No. 15)]|year=1991|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=20–26}} * Other sources: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本歴史地名大系 28 大阪府の地名 (Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place names, Volume 28, Osaka)|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本古墳大辞典 (Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun)|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989}} ==
