Full view of the tumulus | part_of = |location = Okamoto-chō 33 (Azatsuka-no-Mae), {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 51|coordinates = 8.71 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 35|coordinates = 33.35 |omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} |area = Kofun length: 94 m |Construction = 5th century |burial = Unknown (contains a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}} made of tuff) |Interred_individual = Unknown |burial goods = Grave goods, haniwa fragments |Historic Site = None |designation1 = Sarcophagus (Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 cultural property) |notes = |map = Japan Osaka |map_dot_label = Maezuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Maezuka Kofun''' (前塚古墳) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == The Maezuka Kofun was constructed at the southern end of the Bentenyama Hills in northern Takatsuki City, Osaka Prefecture. Over time, the mound has been partially leveled due to agricultural activities, and the surrounding moat has been interrupted by the construction of a road on the south side. Archaeological investigations have uncovered burial goods and remains of the tumulus, including a sarcophagus unearthed during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a short front section (a defining feature of this type of kofun) oriented westward. Fragments of haniwa, including cylindrical, lid-shaped, and house-shaped examples, have been found on the surface of the mound. The surrounding moat is 10–17 meters wide, and the total length of the kofun, including the moat, is estimated to be 120 meters. Although the specific structure of the burial facility is unclear, it is known that a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}} made of tuff was found inside, along with grave goods. Based on the haniwa fragments and sarcophagus design, the Maezuka Kofun is estimated to have been built during the 5th century, placing it in the middleof the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. Previously, it was believed to be an {{ill|Baizuka|lt=accompanying mound|ja|陪塚|12=draft}} of the nearby {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|12=draft}}, but current research classifies it as an earlier kofun. The sarcophagus was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property in 1974. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length (including moat): 120 meters * Kofun length: 94 meters * Rear section (circular): ** Diameter: 69 meters * Front section (rectangular): ** Length: 25 meters ** Width: 25 meters (estimated) The construction plan shows similarities to the nearby {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳|12=draft}} in Takatsuki City. The surrounding moat mirrors the type of the tumulus, measuring 16 meters wide on the rear side and 10 meters on the front side. == burial == [[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun
Designated as an Osaka Prefecture cultural property}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] The burial facility's structure is unknown, but it contained a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}}, which was exposed and discovered during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The sarcophagus, made of tuff, measures approximately 2 meters in length, 0.6 meters in width, and 0.8 meters in height. Its lid features a curved type (so-called "Inrō-lid"), with rope-like protrusions on each side and triangular incisions on the front and back ends. Grave goods such as mirrors, iron swords, and iron halberds were reportedly found, although their details remain unclear. The sarcophagus has been preserved and is Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property. The original sarcophagus is displayed at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while a replica is exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校|12=draft}}. == designation1 == === Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 === * Tangible designation1: ** Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun (archaeological artifact) - Preserved at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 List] (Osaka Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] ({{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町|12=draft}}, Minamikawachi District) - Preserves and exhibits the original sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校|12=draft}} ({{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市|12=draft}}, Shinjōchō) - Displays a replica of the sarcophagus. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Local government publications: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=高槻市史 第6巻 考古編 (Takatsuki City History, Volume 6, Archaeological Edition)|year=1973|publisher=Takatsuki City|pages=59–60}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4900 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要9 (Summary of Excavations, No. 9)]|year=1985|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|page=18}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4644 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要13 (Summary of Excavations, No. 13)]|year=1989|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=19–23}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/5175 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要15 (Summary of Excavations, No. 15)]|year=1991|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=20–26}} * Other sources: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本歴史地名大系 28 大阪府の地名 (Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place names, Volume 28, Osaka)|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本古墳大辞典 (Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun)|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989}} == Its lid features a curved type (so-called "Inrō-lid"), with rope-like protrusions on each side and triangular incisions on the front and back ends. Grave goods such as mirrors, iron swords, and iron halberds were reportedly found, although their details remain unclear. The sarcophagus has been preserved and is Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property. The original sarcophagus is displayed at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while a replica is exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校|12=draft}}. == designation1 == === Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 === * Tangible designation1: ** Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun (archaeological artifact) - Preserved at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 List] (Osaka Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] ({{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町|12=draft}}, Minamikawachi District) - Preserves and exhibits the original sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校|12=draft}} ({{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市|12=draft}}, Shinjōchō) - Displays a replica of the sarcophagus. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Local government publications: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=高槻市史 第6巻 考古編 (Takatsuki City History, Volume 6, Archaeological Edition)|year=1973|publisher=Takatsuki City|pages=59–60}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4900 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要9 (Summary of Excavations, No. 9)]|year=1985|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|page=18}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4644 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要13 (Summary of Excavations, No. 13)]|year=1989|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=19–23}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/5175 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要15 (Summary of Excavations, No. 15)]|year=1991|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=20–26}} * Other sources: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本歴史地名大系 28 大阪府の地名 (Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place names, Volume 28, Osaka)|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本古墳大辞典 (Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun)|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989}} == revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=draft}} |name = Maezuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maezuka Kofun (Takatsuki), zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the tumulus | part_of = |location = Okamoto-chō 33 (Azatsuka-no-Mae), {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 51|coordinates = 8.71 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 35|coordinates = 33.35 |omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} |area = Kofun length: 94 m |Construction = 5th century |burial = Unknown (contains a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}} made of tuff) |Interred_individual = Unknown |burial goods = Grave goods, haniwa fragments |Historic Site = None |designation1 = Sarcophagus (Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 cultural property) |notes = |map = Japan Osaka |map_dot_label = Maezuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Maezuka Kofun''' (前塚古墳) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == The Maezuka Kofun was constructed at the southern end of the Bentenyama Hills in northern Takatsuki City, Osaka Prefecture. Over time, the mound has been partially leveled due to agricultural activities, and the surrounding moat has been interrupted by the construction of a road on the south side. Archaeological investigations have uncovered burial goods and remains of the tumulus, including a sarcophagus unearthed during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a short front section (a defining feature of this type of kofun) oriented westward. Fragments of haniwa, including cylindrical, lid-shaped, and house-shaped examples, have been found on the surface of the mound. The surrounding moat is 10–17 meters wide, and the total length of the kofun, including the moat, is estimated to be 120 meters. Although the specific structure of the burial facility is unclear, it is known that a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}} made of tuff was found inside, along with grave goods. Based on the haniwa fragments and sarcophagus design, the Maezuka Kofun is estimated to have been built during the 5th century, placing it in the middleof the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. Previously, it was believed to be an {{ill|Baizuka|lt=accompanying mound|ja|陪塚|12=draft}} of the nearby {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|12=draft}}, but current research classifies it as an earlier kofun. The sarcophagus was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property in 1974. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length (including moat): 120 meters * Kofun length: 94 meters * Rear section (circular): ** Diameter: 69 meters * Front section (rectangular): ** Length: 25 meters ** Width: 25 meters (estimated) The construction plan shows similarities to the nearby {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳|12=draft}} in Takatsuki City. The surrounding moat mirrors the type of the tumulus, measuring 16 meters wide on the rear side and 10 meters on the front side. == burial == [[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun
Designated as an Osaka Prefecture cultural property}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] The burial facility's structure is unknown, but it contained a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}}, which was exposed and discovered during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The sarcophagus, made of tuff, measures approximately 2 meters in length, 0.6 meters in width, and 0.8 meters in height. Its lid features a curved type (so-called "Inrō-lid"), with rope-like protrusions on each side and triangular incisions on the front and back ends. Grave goods such as mirrors, iron swords, and iron halberds were reportedly found, although their details remain unclear. The sarcophagus has been preserved and is Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property. The original sarcophagus is displayed at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while a replica is exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校|12=draft}}. == designation1 == === Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 === * Tangible designation1: ** Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun (archaeological artifact) - Preserved at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 List] (Osaka Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] ({{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町|12=draft}}, Minamikawachi District) - Preserves and exhibits the original sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校|12=draft}} ({{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市|12=draft}}, Shinjōchō) - Displays a replica of the sarcophagus. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Local government publications: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=高槻市史 第6巻 考古編 (Takatsuki City History, Volume 6, Archaeological Edition)|year=1973|publisher=Takatsuki City|pages=59–60}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4900 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要9 (Summary of Excavations, No. 9)]|year=1985|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|page=18}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4644 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要13 (Summary of Excavations, No. 13)]|year=1989|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=19–23}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/5175 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要15 (Summary of Excavations, No. 15)]|year=1991|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=20–26}} * Other sources: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本歴史地名大系 28 大阪府の地名 (Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place names, Volume 28, Osaka)|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本古墳大辞典 (Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun)|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳|12=draft}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Maetsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and Full view of the tumulus | part_of = |location = Okamoto-chō 33 (Azatsuka-no-Mae), {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 51|coordinates = 8.71 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 35|coordinates = 33.35 |omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} |area = Kofun length: 94 m |Construction = 5th century |burial = Unknown (contains a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}} made of tuff) |Interred_individual = Unknown |burial goods = Grave goods, haniwa fragments |Historic Site = None |designation1 = Sarcophagus (Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 cultural property) |notes = |map = Japan Osaka |map_dot_label = Maezuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Maezuka Kofun''' (前塚古墳) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == The Maezuka Kofun was constructed at the southern end of the Bentenyama Hills in northern Takatsuki City, Osaka Prefecture. Over time, the mound has been partially leveled due to agricultural activities, and the surrounding moat has been interrupted by the construction of a road on the south side. Archaeological investigations have uncovered burial goods and remains of the tumulus, including a sarcophagus unearthed during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a short front section (a defining feature of this type of kofun) oriented westward. Fragments of haniwa, including cylindrical, lid-shaped, and house-shaped examples, have been found on the surface of the mound. The surrounding moat is 10–17 meters wide, and the total length of the kofun, including the moat, is estimated to be 120 meters. Although the specific structure of the burial facility is unclear, it is known that a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}} made of tuff was found inside, along with grave goods. Based on the haniwa fragments and sarcophagus design, the Maezuka Kofun is estimated to have been built during the 5th century, placing it in the middleof the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. Previously, it was believed to be an {{ill|Baizuka|lt=accompanying mound|ja|陪塚|12=draft}} of the nearby {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|12=draft}}, but current research classifies it as an earlier kofun. The sarcophagus was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property in 1974. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length (including moat): 120 meters * Kofun length: 94 meters * Rear section (circular): ** Diameter: 69 meters * Front section (rectangular): ** Length: 25 meters ** Width: 25 meters (estimated) The construction plan shows similarities to the nearby {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳|12=draft}} in Takatsuki City. The surrounding moat mirrors the type of the tumulus, measuring 16 meters wide on the rear side and 10 meters on the front side. == burial == [[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun
Designated as an Osaka Prefecture cultural property}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] The burial facility's structure is unknown, but it contained a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}}, which was exposed and discovered during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The sarcophagus, made of tuff, measures approximately 2 meters in length, 0.6 meters in width, and 0.8 meters in height. Its lid features a curved type (so-called "Inrō-lid"), with rope-like protrusions on each side and triangular incisions on the front and back ends. Grave goods such as mirrors, iron swords, and iron halberds were reportedly found, although their details remain unclear. The sarcophagus has been preserved and is Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property. The original sarcophagus is displayed at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while a replica is exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校|12=draft}}. == designation1 == === Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 === * Tangible designation1: ** Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun (archaeological artifact) - Preserved at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 List] (Osaka Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] ({{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町|12=draft}}, Minamikawachi District) - Preserves and exhibits the original sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校|12=draft}} ({{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市|12=draft}}, Shinjōchō) - Displays a replica of the sarcophagus. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Local government publications: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=高槻市史 第6巻 考古編 (Takatsuki City History, Volume 6, Archaeological Edition)|year=1973|publisher=Takatsuki City|pages=59–60}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4900 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要9 (Summary of Excavations, No. 9)]|year=1985|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|page=18}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4644 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要13 (Summary of Excavations, No. 13)]|year=1989|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=19–23}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/5175 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要15 (Summary of Excavations, No. 15)]|year=1991|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=20–26}} * Other sources: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本歴史地名大系 28 大阪府の地名 (Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place names, Volume 28, Osaka)|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本古墳大辞典 (Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun)|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳|12=draft}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Maetsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Maetsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Maetsuka Kofun]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Maetsuka Kofun]] [[ja:前塚古墳 (高槻市)]] [[ja:前塚古墳]] {{citation needed|Maetsuka Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

Place names, Volume 28, Osaka)|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本古墳大辞典 (Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun)|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989}} == {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=draft}} |name = Maezuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maezuka Kofun (Takatsuki), zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the tumulus | part_of = |location = Okamoto-chō 33 (Azatsuka-no-Mae), {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 51|coordinates = 8.71 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 35|coordinates = 33.35 |omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} |area = Kofun length: 94 m |Construction = 5th century |burial = Unknown (contains a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}} made of tuff) |Interred_individual = Unknown |burial goods = Grave goods, haniwa fragments |Historic Site = None |designation1 = Sarcophagus (Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 cultural property) |notes = |map = Japan Osaka |map_dot_label = Maezuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Maezuka Kofun''' (前塚古墳) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == The Maezuka Kofun was constructed at the southern end of the Bentenyama Hills in northern Takatsuki City, Osaka Prefecture. Over time, the mound has been partially leveled due to agricultural activities, and the surrounding moat has been interrupted by the construction of a road on the south side. Archaeological investigations have uncovered burial goods and remains of the tumulus, including a sarcophagus unearthed during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a short front section (a defining feature of this type of kofun) oriented westward. Fragments of haniwa, including cylindrical, lid-shaped, and house-shaped examples, have been found on the surface of the mound. The surrounding moat is 10–17 meters wide, and the total length of the kofun, including the moat, is estimated to be 120 meters. Although the specific structure of the burial facility is unclear, it is known that a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}} made of tuff was found inside, along with grave goods. Based on the haniwa fragments and sarcophagus design, the Maezuka Kofun is estimated to have been built during the 5th century, placing it in the middleof the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. Previously, it was believed to be an {{ill|Baizuka|lt=accompanying mound|ja|陪塚|12=draft}} of the nearby {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|12=draft}}, but current research classifies it as an earlier kofun. The sarcophagus was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property in 1974. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length (including moat): 120 meters * Kofun length: 94 meters * Rear section (circular): ** Diameter: 69 meters * Front section (rectangular): ** Length: 25 meters ** Width: 25 meters (estimated) The construction plan shows similarities to the nearby {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳|12=draft}} in Takatsuki City. The surrounding moat mirrors the type of the tumulus, measuring 16 meters wide on the rear side and 10 meters on the front side. == burial == [[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun
Designated as an Osaka Prefecture cultural property}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] The burial facility's structure is unknown, but it contained a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}}, which was exposed and discovered during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The sarcophagus, made of tuff, measures approximately 2 meters in length, 0.6 meters in width, and 0.8 meters in height. Its lid features a curved type (so-called "Inrō-lid"), with rope-like protrusions on each side and triangular incisions on the front and back ends. Grave goods such as mirrors, iron swords, and iron halberds were reportedly found, although their details remain unclear. The sarcophagus has been preserved and is Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property. The original sarcophagus is displayed at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while a replica is exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校|12=draft}}. == designation1 == === Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 === * Tangible designation1: ** Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun (archaeological artifact) - Preserved at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 List] (Osaka Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] ({{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町|12=draft}}, Minamikawachi District) - Preserves and exhibits the original sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校|12=draft}} ({{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市|12=draft}}, Shinjōchō) - Displays a replica of the sarcophagus. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Local government publications: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=高槻市史 第6巻 考古編 (Takatsuki City History, Volume 6, Archaeological Edition)|year=1973|publisher=Takatsuki City|pages=59–60}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4900 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要9 (Summary of Excavations, No. 9)]|year=1985|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|page=18}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4644 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要13 (Summary of Excavations, No. 13)]|year=1989|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=19–23}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/5175 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要15 (Summary of Excavations, No. 15)]|year=1991|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=20–26}} * Other sources: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本歴史地名大系 28 大阪府の地名 (Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place names, Volume 28, Osaka)|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本古墳大辞典 (Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun)|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳|12=draft}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Maetsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] The original sarcophagus is displayed at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while a replica is exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校|12=draft|12=draft}}. == designation1 == === Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 === * Tangible designation1: ** Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun (archaeological artifact) - Preserved at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 List] (Osaka Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] ({{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Minamikawachi District) - Displays a replica of the sarcophagus. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Local government publications: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=高槻市史 第6巻 考古編 (Takatsuki City History, Volume 6, Archaeological Edition)|year=1973|publisher=Takatsuki City|pages=59–60}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4900 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要9 (Summary of Excavations, No. 9)]|year=1985|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|page=18}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4644 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要13 (Summary of Excavations, No. 13)]|year=1989|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=19–23}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/5175 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要15 (Summary of Excavations, No. 15)]|year=1991|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=20–26}} * Other sources: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本歴史地名大系 28 大阪府の地名 (Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place names, Volume 28, Osaka)|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本古墳大辞典 (Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun)|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Maetsuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Maetsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takatsuki]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11-1月4日 (旧暦)-dd]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Maetsuka Kofun]] [[ja:前塚古墳 (高槻市)]] [[ja:前塚古墳]]

Full view of the tumulus | part_of = |location = Okamoto-chō 33 (Azatsuka-no-Mae), {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft|12=draft}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 51|coordinates = 8.71 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 35|coordinates = 33.35 |omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} |area = Kofun length: 94 m |built = 5th century |burial = Unknown (contains a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft|12=draft}} made of tuff) |Interred_individual = Unknown |burial goods = Grave goods, haniwa fragments |Historic Site = None |designation1 = Sarcophagus (Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 cultural property) |notes = |map = Japan Osaka |map_dot_label = Maezuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Maezuka Kofun''' (前塚古墳) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == The Maezuka Kofun was constructed at the southern end of the Bentenyama Hills in northern Takatsuki City, Osaka Prefecture. Over time, the mound has been partially leveled due to agricultural activities, and the surrounding moat has been interrupted by the construction of a road on the south side. Archaeological investigations have uncovered burial goods and remains of the tumulus, including a sarcophagus unearthed during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a short front section (a defining feature of this type of kofun) oriented westward. Fragments of haniwa, including cylindrical, lid-shaped, and house-shaped examples, have been found on the surface of the mound. The surrounding moat is 10–17 meters wide, and the total length of the kofun, including the moat, is estimated to be 120 meters. Although the specific structure of the burial facility is unclear, it is known that a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft|12=draft}} made of tuff was found inside, along with grave goods. Based on the haniwa fragments and sarcophagus design, the Maezuka Kofun is estimated to have been built during the 5th century, placing it in the middleof the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. Previously, it was believed to be an {{ill|Baizuka|lt=accompanying mound|ja|陪塚|12=draft|12=draft}} of the nearby {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, but current research classifies it as an earlier kofun. The sarcophagus was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property in 1974. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length (including moat): 120 meters * Kofun length: 94 meters * Rear section (circular): ** Diameter: 69 meters * Front section (rectangular): ** Length: 25 meters ** Width: 25 meters (estimated) The construction plan shows similarities to the nearby {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Takatsuki City. The surrounding moat mirrors the type of the tumulus, measuring 16 meters wide on the rear side and 10 meters on the front side. == burial == [[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun
Designated as an Osaka Prefecture cultural property}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] The burial facility's structure is unknown, but it contained a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft|12=draft}}, which was exposed and discovered during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The sarcophagus, made of tuff, measures approximately 2 meters in length, 0.6 meters in width, and 0.8 meters in height. Its lid features a curved type (so-called "Inrō-lid"), with rope-like protrusions on each side and triangular incisions on the front and back ends. Grave goods such as mirrors, iron swords, and iron halberds were reportedly found, although their details remain unclear. The sarcophagus has been preserved and is Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property. The original sarcophagus is displayed at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while a replica is exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校|12=draft|12=draft}}. == designation1 == === Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 === * Tangible designation1: ** Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun (archaeological artifact) - Preserved at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 List] (Osaka Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] ({{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Minamikawachi District) - Preserves and exhibits the original sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Shinjōchō) - Displays a replica of the sarcophagus. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Local government publications: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=高槻市史 第6巻 考古編 (Takatsuki City History, Volume 6, Archaeological Edition)|year=1973|publisher=Takatsuki City|pages=59–60}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4900 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要9 (Summary of Excavations, No. 9)]|year=1985|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|page=18}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4644 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要13 (Summary of Excavations, No. 13)]|year=1989|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=19–23}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/5175 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要15 (Summary of Excavations, No. 15)]|year=1991|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=20–26}} * Other sources: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本歴史地名大系 28 大阪府の地名 (Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place names, Volume 28, Osaka)|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本古墳大辞典 (Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun)|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Maetsuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Maetsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takatsuki]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11-1月4日 (旧暦)-dd]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Maetsuka Kofun]] [[ja:前塚古墳 (高槻市)]] [[ja:前塚古墳]]

Based on the haniwa fragments and sarcophagus design, the Maezuka Kofun is estimated to have been built during the 5th century, placing it in the middleof the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Previously, it was believed to be an {{ill|Baizuka|lt=accompanying mound|ja|陪塚}} of the nearby {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}}, but current research classifies it as an earlier kofun. The sarcophagus was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property in 1974. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length (including moat): 120 meters * Kofun length: 94 meters * Rear section (circular): ** Diameter: 69 meters * Front section (rectangular): ** Length: 25 meters ** Width: 25 meters (estimated) The construction plan shows similarities to the nearby {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳}} in Takatsuki City. The surrounding moat mirrors the type of the tumulus, measuring 16 meters wide on the rear side and 10 meters on the front side. == burial == [[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun
Designated as an Osaka Prefecture cultural property}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] The burial facility's structure is unknown, but it contained a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}, which was exposed and discovered during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The sarcophagus, made of tuff, measures approximately 2 meters in length, 0.6 meters in width, and 0.8 meters in height. Its lid features a curved type (so-called "Inrō-lid"), with rope-like protrusions on each side and triangular incisions on the front and back ends. Grave goods such as mirrors, iron swords, and iron halberds were reportedly found, although their details remain unclear. The sarcophagus has been preserved and is Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property. The original sarcophagus is displayed at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while a replica is exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校}}. == designation1 == === Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 === * Tangible designation1: ** Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun (archaeological artifact) - Preserved at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 List] (Osaka Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] ({{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, Minamikawachi District) - Preserves and exhibits the original sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校}} ({{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Shinjōchō) - Displays a replica of the sarcophagus. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Local government publications: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=高槻市史 第6巻 考古編 (Takatsuki City History, Volume 6, Archaeological Edition)|year=1973|publisher=Takatsuki City|pages=59–60}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4900 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要9 (Summary of Excavations, No. 9)]|year=1985|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|page=18}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4644 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要13 (Summary of Excavations, No. 13)]|year=1989|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=19–23}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/5175 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要15 (Summary of Excavations, No. 15)]|year=1991|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=20–26}} * Other sources: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本歴史地名大系 28 大阪府の地名 (Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place names, Volume 28, Osaka)|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本古墳大辞典 (Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun)|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989}} == Related Articles == * infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Maetsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takatsuki]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun Full view of the tumulus | part_of = |location = Okamoto-chō 33 (Azatsuka-no-Mae), {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 51|coordinates = 8.71 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 35|coordinates = 33.35 |omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |area = Kofun length: 94 m |built = 5th century |burial = Unknown (contains a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} made of tuff) |Interred_individual = Unknown |burial goods = Grave goods, haniwa fragments |Historic Site = None |designation1 = Sarcophagus (Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 cultural property) |notes = |map = Japan Osaka |map_dot_label = Maezuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Maezuka Kofun''' (前塚古墳) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == The Maezuka Kofun was constructed at the southern end of the Bentenyama Hills in northern Takatsuki City, Osaka Prefecture. Over time, the mound has been partially leveled due to agricultural activities, and the surrounding moat has been interrupted by the construction of a road on the south side. Archaeological investigations have uncovered burial goods and remains of the tumulus, including a sarcophagus unearthed during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a short front section (a defining feature of this type of kofun) oriented westward. Fragments of haniwa, including cylindrical, lid-shaped, and house-shaped examples, have been found on the surface of the mound. The surrounding moat is 10–17 meters wide, and the total length of the kofun, including the moat, is estimated to be 120 meters. Although the specific structure of the burial facility is unclear, it is known that a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} made of tuff was found inside, along with grave goods. Based on the haniwa fragments and sarcophagus design, the Maezuka Kofun is estimated to have been built during the 5th century, placing it in the middleof the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Previously, it was believed to be an {{ill|Baizuka|lt=accompanying mound|ja|陪塚}} of the nearby {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}}, but current research classifies it as an earlier kofun. The sarcophagus was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property in 1974. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length (including moat): 120 meters * Kofun length: 94 meters * Rear section (circular): ** Diameter: 69 meters * Front section (rectangular): ** Length: 25 meters ** Width: 25 meters (estimated) The construction plan shows similarities to the nearby {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳}} in Takatsuki City. The surrounding moat mirrors the type of the tumulus, measuring 16 meters wide on the rear side and 10 meters on the front side. == burial == [[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun
Designated as an Osaka Prefecture cultural property}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] The burial facility's structure is unknown, but it contained a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}, which was exposed and discovered during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The sarcophagus, made of tuff, measures approximately 2 meters in length, 0.6 meters in width, and 0.8 meters in height. Its lid features a curved type (so-called "Inrō-lid"), with rope-like protrusions on each side and triangular incisions on the front and back ends. Grave goods such as mirrors, iron swords, and iron halberds were reportedly found, although their details remain unclear. The sarcophagus has been preserved and is Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property. The original sarcophagus is displayed at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while a replica is exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校}}. == designation1 == === Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 === * Tangible designation1: ** Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun (archaeological artifact) - Preserved at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 List] (Osaka Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] ({{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, Minamikawachi District) - Preserves and exhibits the original sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校}} ({{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Shinjōchō) - Displays a replica of the sarcophagus. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Local government publications: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=高槻市史 第6巻 考古編 (Takatsuki City History, Volume 6, Archaeological Edition)|year=1973|publisher=Takatsuki City|pages=59–60}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4900 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要9 (Summary of Excavations, No. 9)]|year=1985|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|page=18}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4644 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要13 (Summary of Excavations, No. 13)]|year=1989|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=19–23}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/5175 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要15 (Summary of Excavations, No. 15)]|year=1991|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=20–26}} * Other sources: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本歴史地名大系 28 大阪府の地名 (Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place names, Volume 28, Osaka)|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本古墳大辞典 (Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun)|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Maetsuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Maetsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takatsuki]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Maetsuka Kofun]] [[ja:前塚古墳 (高槻市)]] [[ja:前塚古墳]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 前塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Maezuka Kofun (Takatsuki), zenkei.jpg|280px]]
墳丘全景 |所属 = |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[高槻市]]岡本町33（字塚ノ前） |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 51|緯度秒 = 8.71 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 35|経度秒 = 33.35 |ISO = JP-27 |形状 = [[帆立貝形古墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長94m |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]代 |埋葬施設 = 不明（内部に[[凝灰岩]]製[[長持形石棺]]） |被葬者 = |出土品 = 副葬品・[[埴輪]]片 |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = 石棺（大阪府指定文化財） |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Osaka |ラベル = 前塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''前塚古墳'''（まえづかこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[高槻市]]岡本町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[帆立貝形古墳]]。史跡指定はされていない。 == 概要 == 大阪府北部、高槻市北部の弁天山丘陵を南に降りきった地に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|前塚古墳（平凡社）|1986}}。現在までに開墾等により墳丘が一部削平を受けているほか、墳丘南側に引かれた道路により周濠が分断されている{{Sfn|嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要15|1991|pp=20-26}}。考古学的には、[[明治]]期の開墾によって石棺が出土したことが知られるほか{{Sfn|高槻市文化財年報 平成11年度|2001|pp=8-11}}、これまでに墳丘周囲で数次の発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は前方部が短小な帆立貝形の前方後円形で、前方部を西方に向ける{{Sfn|前塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}{{Sfn|高槻市文化財年報 平成11年度|2001|pp=8-11}}。墳丘外表では[[葺石]]・[[埴輪]]片（[[円筒埴輪]]・蓋形埴輪・家形埴輪）が検出されている{{Sfn|高槻市文化財年報 平成11年度|2001|pp=8-11}}。また墳丘周囲には幅10-17メートルの周濠が巡らされており、周濠を含めた古墳総長は120メートルにおよぶ{{Sfn|嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要13|1989|pp=19-23}}。埋葬施設の構造は詳らかでないが、前述の明治期の開墾の際に[[凝灰岩]]製[[長持形石棺]]が出土したことが知られ、その際には副葬品も出土したという{{Sfn|嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要15|1991|pp=20-26}}。 この前塚古墳は、出土埴輪・出土石棺より[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]頃（5世紀初頭{{Sfn|嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要13|1989|pp=19-23}}または5世紀中葉-後半{{Sfn|前塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}）の築造と推定される。かつては近在の[[今城塚古墳]]の[[陪塚]]とする説もあったが、現在は先行古墳に位置づけられる{{Sfn|前塚古墳（平凡社）|1986}}。 出土石棺は[[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）に大阪府指定有形文化財に指定されている。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要13|1989|pp=19-23}}。 * 古墳総長：120メートル - 周濠を含めた全長。 * 墳丘長：94メートル * 後円部 ** 直径：69メートル * 前方部 ** 長さ：25メートル ** 幅：25メートル（推定値） 築造企画は、西方の[[番山古墳]]（高槻市土室町）との類似が指摘される{{Sfn|前塚古墳（平凡社）|1986}}{{Sfn|前塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。墳丘周囲に巡らされている周濠は墳丘と相似形で、幅は後円部側で16メートル、前方部側で10メートルを測る{{Sfn|嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要13|1989|pp=19-23}}。 == 埋葬施設 == [[ファイル:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|前塚古墳出土 [[長持形石棺]]
（大阪府指定文化財）}}{{small|[[大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館]]展示。}}]] 埋葬施設の構造は不明であるが、施設内部に[[長持形石棺]]が納められていたことが知られる{{Sfn|嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要15|1991|pp=20-26}}。この石棺は明治期の開墾の際に露出して発見されたもので、[[凝灰岩]]製であり、長さ約2メートル・幅約0.6メートル・高さ約0.8メートルを測る{{Sfn|嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要13|1989|pp=19-23}}。蓋はいわゆる印籠蓋の形状で蒲鉾状を呈し、側石には各1個の縄掛突起を有する。突起のある前後両端には三角形の沈刻文も認められている。また副葬品として、鏡・鉄刀・鉄鉾の存在が知られるが、詳細は明らかでない{{Sfn|嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要13|1989|pp=19-23}}。 この石棺は大阪府指定有形文化財に指定されている。実物は長らく[[大阪府立茨木高等学校]]に置かれていたが、現在は[[大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館]]において保存・展示されている。また、レプリカが大阪府立茨木高等学校に展示されている。 == 文化財 == === 大阪府指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 前塚古墳石棺（考古資料） - [[大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館]]保管。1974年（昭和49年）3月29日指定[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html 大阪府内指定文化財一覧表]（大阪府ホームページ）の河南町ファイルより。。 == 関連施設 == * [[大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館]]（[[南河内郡]][[河南町]]） - 前塚古墳の出土石棺（実物）を保管・展示。 * [[大阪府立茨木高等学校]]（[[茨木市]]新庄町） - 前塚古墳の出土石棺レプリカを展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 地方自治体発行 ** 「前塚古墳」『高槻市史 第6巻 考古編』 高槻市史編さん委員会編、高槻市、1973年、59-60頁 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1985|chapter=前塚古墳|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4900 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要9（高槻市文化財調査概要IX）]|publisher=高槻市教育委員会社会教育部社会教育課|isbn=|page=18|ref={{Harvid|嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要9|1985}}}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=前塚古墳|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4644 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要13（高槻市文化財調査概要XIII）]|publisher=高槻市教育委員会社会教育部社会教育課・高槻市立埋蔵文化財調査センター|isbn=|pages=19-23|ref={{Harvid|嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要13|1989}}}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=前塚古墳|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/5175 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要15（高槻市文化財調査概要XVI）]|publisher=高槻市教育委員会・高槻市立埋蔵文化財調査センター|isbn=|pages=20-26|ref={{Harvid|嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要15|1991}}}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2001|chapter=前塚古墳の調査|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/5138 高槻市文化財年報 平成11年度]|publisher=高槻市教育委員会文化財課埋蔵文化財調査センター|isbn=|pages=8-11|ref={{Harvid|高槻市文化財年報 平成11年度|2001}}}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * その他 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=前塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 28 大阪府の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|前塚古墳（平凡社）|1986}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=高島徹|year=1989|chapter=前塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|前塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[番山古墳]] {{デフォルトソート:まえつかこふん たかつきし}} [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:高槻市の歴史]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[前塚古墳 (高槻市)]] to [[Maetsuka Kofun]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

（大阪府指定文化財）}}{{small|[[大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館]]展示。}}]] 埋葬施設の構造は不明であるが、施設内部に[[長持形石棺]]が納められていたことが知られる{{Sfn|嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要15|1991|pp=20-26}}。この石棺は明治期の開墾の際に露出して発見されたもので、[[凝灰岩]]製であり、長さ約2メートル・幅約0.6メートル・高さ約0.8メートルを測る{{Sfn|嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要13|1989|pp=19-23}}。蓋はいわゆる印籠蓋の形状で蒲鉾状を呈し、側石には各1個の縄掛突起を有する。突起のある前後両端には三角形の沈刻文も認められている。また副葬品として、鏡・鉄刀・鉄鉾の存在が知られるが、詳細は明らかでない{{Sfn|嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要13|1989|pp=19-23}}。 この石棺は大阪府指定有形文化財に指定されている。実物は長らく[[大阪府立茨木高等学校]]に置かれていたが、現在は[[大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館]]において保存・展示されている。また、レプリカが大阪府立茨木高等学校に展示されている。 == 文化財 == === 大阪府指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 前塚古墳石棺（考古資料） - [[大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館]]保管。1974年（昭和49年）3月29日指定[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html 大阪府内指定文化財一覧表]（大阪府ホームページ）の河南町ファイルより。。 == 関連施設 == * [[大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館]]（[[南河内郡]][[河南町]]） - 前塚古墳の出土石棺（実物）を保管・展示。 * [[大阪府立茨木高等学校]]（[[茨木市]]新庄町） - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * その他 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=前塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 28 大阪府の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=458249028X|ref={{Harvid|前塚古墳（平凡社）|1986}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=高島徹|year=1989|chapter=前塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|前塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[番山古墳]] {{デフォルトソート:まえつかこふん たかつきし}} [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:高槻市の歴史]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]]

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. Full view of the tumulus | part_of = |location = Okamoto-chō 33 (Azatsuka-no-Mae), {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 51|coordinates = 8.71 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 35|coordinates = 33.35 |omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} |area = Kofun length: 94 m |built = 5th century |burial = Unknown (contains a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} made of tuff) |Interred_individual = Unknown |burial goods = Grave goods, haniwa fragments |Historic Site = None |designation1 = Sarcophagus (Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 cultural property) |notes = |map = Japan Osaka |map_dot_label = Maezuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Maezuka Kofun''' (前塚古墳) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == The Maezuka Kofun was constructed at the southern end of the Bentenyama Hills in northern Takatsuki City, Osaka Prefecture. Over time, the mound has been partially leveled due to agricultural activities, and the surrounding moat has been interrupted by the construction of a road on the south side. Archaeological investigations have uncovered burial goods and remains of the tumulus, including a sarcophagus unearthed during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a short front section (a defining feature of this type of kofun) oriented westward. Fragments of haniwa, including cylindrical, lid-shaped, and house-shaped examples, have been found on the surface of the mound. The surrounding moat is 10–17 meters wide, and the total length of the kofun, including the moat, is estimated to be 120 meters. Although the specific structure of the burial facility is unclear, it is known that a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}} made of tuff was found inside, along with grave goods. Based on the haniwa fragments and sarcophagus design, the Maezuka Kofun is estimated to have been built during the 5th century, placing it in the middleof the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Previously, it was believed to be an {{ill|Baizuka|lt=accompanying mound|ja|陪塚}} of the nearby {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳}}, but current research classifies it as an earlier kofun. The sarcophagus was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property in 1974. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length (including moat): 120 meters * Kofun length: 94 meters * Rear section (circular): ** Diameter: 69 meters * Front section (rectangular): ** Length: 25 meters ** Width: 25 meters (estimated) The construction plan shows similarities to the nearby {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳}} in Takatsuki City. The surrounding moat mirrors the type of the tumulus, measuring 16 meters wide on the rear side and 10 meters on the front side. == burial == [[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun
Designated as an Osaka Prefecture cultural property}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] The burial facility's structure is unknown, but it contained a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}, which was exposed and discovered during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The sarcophagus, made of tuff, measures approximately 2 meters in length, 0.6 meters in width, and 0.8 meters in height. Its lid features a curved type (so-called "Inrō-lid"), with rope-like protrusions on each side and triangular incisions on the front and back ends. Grave goods such as mirrors, iron swords, and iron halberds were reportedly found, although their details remain unclear. The sarcophagus has been preserved and is Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property. The original sarcophagus is displayed at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while a replica is exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校}}. == designation1 == === Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 === * Tangible designation1: ** Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun (archaeological artifact) - Preserved at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 List] (Osaka Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] ({{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町}}, Minamikawachi District) - Preserves and exhibits the original sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校}} ({{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市}}, Shinjōchō) - Displays a replica of the sarcophagus. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Local government publications: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=高槻市史 第6巻 考古編 (Takatsuki City History, Volume 6, Archaeological Edition)|year=1973|publisher=Takatsuki City|pages=59–60}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4900 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要9 (Summary of Excavations, No. 9)]|year=1985|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|page=18}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4644 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要13 (Summary of Excavations, No. 13)]|year=1989|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=19–23}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/5175 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要15 (Summary of Excavations, No. 15)]|year=1991|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=20–26}} * Other sources: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本歴史地名大系 28 大阪府の地名 (Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place names, Volume 28, Osaka)|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本古墳大辞典 (Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun)|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989}} == Related Articles == * Full view of the tumulus | part_of = |location = Okamoto-chō 33 (Azatsuka-no-Mae), {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft|12=draft}} |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 51|coordinates = 8.71 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 35|coordinates = 33.35 |omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} |area = Kofun length: 94 m |built = 5th century |burial = Unknown (contains a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft|12=draft}} made of tuff) |Interred_individual = Unknown |burial goods = Grave goods, haniwa fragments |Historic Site = None |designation1 = Sarcophagus (Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 cultural property) |notes = |map = Japan Osaka |map_dot_label = Maezuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Maezuka Kofun''' (前塚古墳) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Takatsuki, Osaka|ja|高槻市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == The Maezuka Kofun was constructed at the southern end of the Bentenyama Hills in northern Takatsuki City, Osaka Prefecture. Over time, the mound has been partially leveled due to agricultural activities, and the surrounding moat has been interrupted by the construction of a road on the south side. Archaeological investigations have uncovered burial goods and remains of the tumulus, including a sarcophagus unearthed during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a short front section (a defining feature of this type of kofun) oriented westward. Fragments of haniwa, including cylindrical, lid-shaped, and house-shaped examples, have been found on the surface of the mound. The surrounding moat is 10–17 meters wide, and the total length of the kofun, including the moat, is estimated to be 120 meters. Although the specific structure of the burial facility is unclear, it is known that a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft|12=draft}} made of tuff was found inside, along with grave goods. Based on the haniwa fragments and sarcophagus design, the Maezuka Kofun is estimated to have been built during the 5th century, placing it in the middleof the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. Previously, it was believed to be an {{ill|Baizuka|lt=accompanying mound|ja|陪塚|12=draft|12=draft}} of the nearby {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|ja|今城塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, but current research classifies it as an earlier kofun. The sarcophagus was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property in 1974. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length (including moat): 120 meters * Kofun length: 94 meters * Rear section (circular): ** Diameter: 69 meters * Front section (rectangular): ** Length: 25 meters ** Width: 25 meters (estimated) The construction plan shows similarities to the nearby {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Takatsuki City. The surrounding moat mirrors the type of the tumulus, measuring 16 meters wide on the rear side and 10 meters on the front side. == burial == [[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun
Designated as an Osaka Prefecture cultural property}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] The burial facility's structure is unknown, but it contained a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft|12=draft}}, which was exposed and discovered during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The sarcophagus, made of tuff, measures approximately 2 meters in length, 0.6 meters in width, and 0.8 meters in height. Its lid features a curved type (so-called "Inrō-lid"), with rope-like protrusions on each side and triangular incisions on the front and back ends. Grave goods such as mirrors, iron swords, and iron halberds were reportedly found, although their details remain unclear. The sarcophagus has been preserved and is Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property. The original sarcophagus is displayed at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while a replica is exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校|12=draft|12=draft}}. == designation1 == === Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 === * Tangible designation1: ** Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun (archaeological artifact) - Preserved at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 List] (Osaka Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] ({{ill|Kanan, Osaka|ja|河南町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Minamikawachi District) - Preserves and exhibits the original sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|ja|茨木市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Shinjōchō) - Displays a replica of the sarcophagus. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Local government publications: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=高槻市史 第6巻 考古編 (Takatsuki City History, Volume 6, Archaeological Edition)|year=1973|publisher=Takatsuki City|pages=59–60}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4900 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要9 (Summary of Excavations, No. 9)]|year=1985|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|page=18}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4644 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要13 (Summary of Excavations, No. 13)]|year=1989|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=19–23}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/5175 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要15 (Summary of Excavations, No. 15)]|year=1991|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=20–26}} * Other sources: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本歴史地名大系 28 大阪府の地名 (Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place names, Volume 28, Osaka)|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本古墳大辞典 (Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun)|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} {{citation needed|Maetsuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|前塚古墳 (高槻市)|version=93995654|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Maetsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takatsuki]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Maetsuka Kofun]] [[ja:前塚古墳 (高槻市)]] [[ja:前塚古墳]]
