Its lid features a curved type (so-called "Inrō-lid"), with rope-like protrusions on each side and triangular incisions on the front and back ends. Grave goods such as mirrors, iron swords, and iron halberds were reportedly found, although their details remain unclear. The sarcophagus has been preserved and is Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property. The original sarcophagus is displayed at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while a replica is exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|qq=}}. == designation1 == === Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 === * Tangible designation1: ** Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun (archaeological artifact) - Preserved at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 List] (Osaka Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] ([[:en:Kanan, Osaka|Kanan, Osaka]], Minamikawachi District) - Preserves and exhibits the original sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|qq=}} ([[:en:Ibaraki, Osaka|Ibaraki, Osaka]], Shinjōchō) - Displays a replica of the sarcophagus. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Local government publications: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=高槻市史 第6巻 考古編 (Takatsuki City History, Volume 6, Archaeological Edition)|year=1973|publisher=Takatsuki City|pages=59–60}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4900 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要9 (Summary of Excavations, No. 9)]|year=1985|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|page=18}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4644 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要13 (Summary of Excavations, No. 13)]|year=1989|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=19–23}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/5175 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要15 (Summary of Excavations, No. 15)]|year=1991|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=20–26}} * Other sources: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本歴史地名大系 28 大阪府の地名 (Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place names, Volume 28, Osaka)|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本古墳大辞典 (Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun)|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banzan Kofun|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|前塚古墳 (高槻市)|version=93995654|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takatsuki]] [[Category:scallop-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Maetsuka Kofun]] [[ja:前塚古墳 (高槻市)]] [[ja:前塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takatsuki]] [[zh:前塚古墳]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11397093]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Maezuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maezuka Kofun (Takatsuki), zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the tumulus | part_of = |location = Okamoto-chō 33 (Azatsuka-no-Mae), [[:en:Takatsuki|Takatsuki, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] |coordinates = {{Coord|34.852419|N|135.592597|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Kofun length: 94 m |built = 5th century |burial = Unknown (contains a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=}} made of tuff) |Interred_individual = Unknown |burial goods = Grave goods, haniwa fragments |Historic Site = None |designation1 = Sarcophagus (Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 cultural property) |notes = |map = Japan Osaka |map_dot_label = Maezuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|qid=Q11480883}} }} '''Maezuka Kofun''' (前塚古墳) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Takatsuki|Takatsuki, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == The Maezuka Kofun was constructed at the southern end of the Bentenyama Hills in northern Takatsuki City, Osaka Prefecture. Over time, the mound has been partially leveled due to agricultural activities, and the surrounding moat has been interrupted by the construction of a road on the south side. Archaeological investigations have uncovered burial goods and remains of the tumulus, including a sarcophagus unearthed during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a short front section (a defining feature of this type of kofun) oriented westward. Fragments of haniwa, including cylindrical, lid-shaped, and house-shaped examples, have been found on the surface of the mound. The surrounding moat is 10–17 meters wide, and the total length of the kofun, including the moat, is estimated to be 120 meters. Although the specific structure of the burial facility is unclear, it is known that a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=}} made of tuff was found inside, along with grave goods. Based on the haniwa fragments and sarcophagus design, the Maezuka Kofun is estimated to have been built during the 5th century, placing it in the middleof the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Previously, it was believed to be an {{ill|Baizuka|lt=accompanying mound|ja|陪塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} of the nearby [[:en:Imashirozuka Kofun|Imashirozuka Kofun]], but current research classifies it as an earlier kofun. The sarcophagus was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property in 1974. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length (including moat): 120 meters * Kofun length: 94 meters * Rear section (circular): ** Diameter: 69 meters * Front section (rectangular): ** Length: 25 meters ** Width: 25 meters (estimated) The construction plan shows similarities to the nearby {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banzan Kofun|qq=}} in Takatsuki City. The surrounding moat mirrors the type of the tumulus, measuring 16 meters wide on the rear side and 10 meters on the front side. == burial == [[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun
Designated as an Osaka Prefecture cultural property}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] The burial facility's structure is unknown, but it contained a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=}}, which was exposed and discovered during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The sarcophagus, made of tuff, measures approximately 2 meters in length, 0.6 meters in width, and 0.8 meters in height. Its lid features a curved type (so-called "Inrō-lid"), with rope-like protrusions on each side and triangular incisions on the front and back ends. Grave goods such as mirrors, iron swords, and iron halberds were reportedly found, although their details remain unclear. The sarcophagus has been preserved and is Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property. The original sarcophagus is displayed at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while a replica is exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|qq=}}. == designation1 == === Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 === * Tangible designation1: ** Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun (archaeological artifact) - Preserved at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 List] (Osaka Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] ([[:en:Kanan, Osaka|Kanan, Osaka]], Minamikawachi District) - Preserves and exhibits the original sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|qq=}} ([[:en:Ibaraki, Osaka|Ibaraki, Osaka]], Shinjōchō) - Displays a replica of the sarcophagus. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Local government publications: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=高槻市史 第6巻 考古編 (Takatsuki City History, Volume 6, Archaeological Edition)|year=1973|publisher=Takatsuki City|pages=59–60}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4900 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要9 (Summary of Excavations, No. 9)]|year=1985|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|page=18}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4644 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要13 (Summary of Excavations, No. 13)]|year=1989|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=19–23}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/5175 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要15 (Summary of Excavations, No. 15)]|year=1991|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=20–26}} * Other sources: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本歴史地名大系 28 大阪府の地名 (Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place names, Volume 28, Osaka)|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本古墳大辞典 (Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun)|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banzan Kofun|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|前塚古墳 (高槻市)|version=93995654|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takatsuki]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Maetsuka Kofun]] [[ja:前塚古墳 (高槻市)]] [[ja:前塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takatsuki]] [[zh:前塚古墳]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

Full view of the tumulus | part_of = |location = Okamoto-chō 33 (Azatsuka-no-Mae), [[:en:Takatsuki|Takatsuki, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] |coordinates = {{Coord|34.852419|N|135.592597|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Kofun length: 94 m |built = 5th century |burial = Unknown (contains a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped stone coffin|qq=}} made of tuff) |Interred_individual = Unknown |burial goods = Grave goods, haniwa fragments |Historic Site = None |designation1 = Sarcophagus (Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 cultural property) |notes = |map = Japan Osaka |map_dot_label = Maezuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|qid=Q11480883}} }} '''Maezuka Kofun''' (前塚古墳) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Takatsuki|Takatsuki, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == The Maezuka Kofun was constructed at the southern end of the Bentenyama Hills in northern Takatsuki City, Osaka Prefecture. Over time, the mound has been partially leveled due to agricultural activities, and the surrounding moat has been interrupted by the construction of a road on the south side. Archaeological investigations have uncovered burial goods and remains of the tumulus, including a sarcophagus unearthed during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a short front section (a defining feature of this type of kofun) oriented westward. Fragments of haniwa, including cylindrical, lid-shaped, and house-shaped examples, have been found on the surface of the mound. The surrounding moat is 10–17 meters wide, and the total length of the kofun, including the moat, is estimated to be 120 meters. Although the specific structure of the burial facility is unclear, it is known that a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped stone coffin|qq=}} made of tuff was found inside, along with grave goods. Based on the haniwa fragments and sarcophagus design, the Maezuka Kofun is estimated to have been built during the 5th century, placing it in the middleof the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Previously, it was believed to be an {{ill|Baizuka|lt=accompanying mound|ja|陪塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} of the nearby [[:en:Imashirozuka Kofun|Imashirozuka Kofun]], but current research classifies it as an earlier kofun. The sarcophagus was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property in 1974. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length (including moat): 120 meters * Kofun length: 94 meters * Rear section (circular): ** Diameter: 69 meters * Front section (rectangular): ** Length: 25 meters ** Width: 25 meters (estimated) The construction plan shows similarities to the nearby {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banzan Kofun|qq=}} in Takatsuki City. The surrounding moat mirrors the type of the tumulus, measuring 16 meters wide on the rear side and 10 meters on the front side. == burial == [[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun
Designated as an Osaka Prefecture cultural property}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] The burial facility's structure is unknown, but it contained a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped stone coffin|qq=}}, which was exposed and discovered during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The sarcophagus, made of tuff, measures approximately 2 meters in length, 0.6 meters in width, and 0.8 meters in height. Its lid features a curved type (so-called "Inrō-lid"), with rope-like protrusions on each side and triangular incisions on the front and back ends. Grave goods such as mirrors, iron swords, and iron halberds were reportedly found, although their details remain unclear. The sarcophagus has been preserved and is Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property. The original sarcophagus is displayed at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while a replica is exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|qq=}}. == designation1 == === Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 === * Tangible designation1: ** Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun (archaeological artifact) - Preserved at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 List] (Osaka Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] ([[:en:Kanan, Osaka|Kanan, Osaka]], Minamikawachi District) - Preserves and exhibits the original sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|qq=}} ([[:en:Ibaraki, Osaka|Ibaraki, Osaka]], Shinjōchō) - Displays a replica of the sarcophagus. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Local government publications: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=高槻市史 第6巻 考古編 (Takatsuki City History, Volume 6, Archaeological Edition)|year=1973|publisher=Takatsuki City|pages=59–60}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4900 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要9 (Summary of Excavations, No. 9)]|year=1985|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|page=18}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4644 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要13 (Summary of Excavations, No. 13)]|year=1989|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=19–23}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/5175 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要15 (Summary of Excavations, No. 15)]|year=1991|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=20–26}} * Other sources: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本歴史地名大系 28 大阪府の地名 (Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place names, Volume 28, Osaka)|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本古墳大辞典 (Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun)|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banzan Kofun|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|前塚古墳 (高槻市)|version=93995654|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takatsuki]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Maetsuka Kofun]] [[ja:前塚古墳 (高槻市)]] [[ja:前塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takatsuki]] [[zh:前塚古墳]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Maetsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]]

Full view of the tumulus | part_of = |location = Okamoto-chō 33 (Azatsuka-no-Mae), [[:en:Takatsuki|Takatsuki, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] |coordinates = {{Coord|34.852419|N|135.592597|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Kofun length: 94 m |built = 5th century |burial = Unknown (contains a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped stone coffin|qq=}} made of tuff) |Interred_individual = Unknown |burial goods = Grave goods, haniwa fragments |Historic Site = None |designation1 = Sarcophagus (Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 cultural property) |notes = |map = Japan Osaka |map_dot_label = Maezuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|qid=Q11480883}} }} '''Maezuka Kofun''' (前塚古墳) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Takatsuki|Takatsuki, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == The Maezuka Kofun was constructed at the southern end of the Bentenyama Hills in northern Takatsuki City, Osaka Prefecture. Over time, the mound has been partially leveled due to agricultural activities, and the surrounding moat has been interrupted by the construction of a road on the south side. Archaeological investigations have uncovered burial goods and remains of the tumulus, including a sarcophagus unearthed during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a short front section (a defining feature of this type of kofun) oriented westward. Fragments of haniwa, including cylindrical, lid-shaped, and house-shaped examples, have been found on the surface of the mound. The surrounding moat is 10–17 meters wide, and the total length of the kofun, including the moat, is estimated to be 120 meters. Although the specific structure of the burial facility is unclear, it is known that a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped stone coffin|qq=}} made of tuff was found inside, along with grave goods. Based on the haniwa fragments and sarcophagus design, the Maezuka Kofun is estimated to have been built during the 5th century, placing it in the middleof the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Previously, it was believed to be an {{ill|Baizuka|lt=accompanying mound|ja|陪塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} of the nearby [[:en:Imashirozuka Kofun|Imashirozuka Kofun]], but current research classifies it as an earlier kofun. The sarcophagus was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property in 1974. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length (including moat): 120 meters * Kofun length: 94 meters * Rear section (circular): ** Diameter: 69 meters * Front section (rectangular): ** Length: 25 meters ** Width: 25 meters (estimated) The construction plan shows similarities to the nearby {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banzan Kofun|qq=}} in Takatsuki City. The surrounding moat mirrors the type of the tumulus, measuring 16 meters wide on the rear side and 10 meters on the front side. == burial == [[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun
Designated as an Osaka Prefecture cultural property}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] The burial facility's structure is unknown, but it contained a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped stone coffin|qq=}}, which was exposed and discovered during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The sarcophagus, made of tuff, measures approximately 2 meters in length, 0.6 meters in width, and 0.8 meters in height. Its lid features a curved type (so-called "Inrō-lid"), with rope-like protrusions on each side and triangular incisions on the front and back ends. Grave goods such as mirrors, iron swords, and iron halberds were reportedly found, although their details remain unclear. The sarcophagus has been preserved and is Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property. The original sarcophagus is displayed at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while a replica is exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|qq=|1=Osaka Ibaraki High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441161}}. == designation1 == === Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 === * Tangible designation1: ** Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun (archaeological artifact) - Preserved at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 List] (Osaka Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] ([[:en:Kanan, Osaka|Kanan, Osaka]], Minamikawachi District) - Preserves and exhibits the original sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|qq=|1=Osaka Ibaraki High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441161}} ([[:en:Ibaraki, Osaka|Ibaraki, Osaka]], Shinjōchō) - Displays a replica of the sarcophagus. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Local government publications: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=高槻市史 第6巻 考古編 (Takatsuki City History, Volume 6, Archaeological Edition)|year=1973|publisher=Takatsuki City|pages=59–60}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4900 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要9 (Summary of Excavations, No. 9)]|year=1985|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|page=18}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4644 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要13 (Summary of Excavations, No. 13)]|year=1989|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=19–23}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/5175 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要15 (Summary of Excavations, No. 15)]|year=1991|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=20–26}} * Other sources: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本歴史地名大系 28 大阪府の地名 (Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place names, Volume 28, Osaka)|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本古墳大辞典 (Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun)|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banzan Kofun|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|前塚古墳 (高槻市)|version=93995654|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takatsuki]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Maetsuka Kofun]] [[ja:前塚古墳 (高槻市)]] [[ja:前塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takatsuki]] [[zh:前塚古墳]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[en:Maetsuka Kofun]]

The sarcophagus was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property in 1974. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length (including moat): 120 meters * Kofun length: 94 meters * Rear section (circular): ** Diameter: 69 meters * Front section (rectangular): ** Length: 25 meters ** Width: 25 meters (estimated) The construction plan shows similarities to the nearby {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banzan Kofun|qq=}} in Takatsuki City. The surrounding moat mirrors the type of the tumulus, measuring 16 meters wide on the rear side and 10 meters on the front side. == burial == [[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun
Designated as an Osaka Prefecture cultural property}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] The burial facility's structure is unknown, but it contained a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped stone coffin|qq=}}, which was exposed and discovered during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The sarcophagus, made of tuff, measures approximately 2 meters in length, 0.6 meters in width, and 0.8 meters in height. Its lid features a curved type (so-called "Inrō-lid"), with rope-like protrusions on each side and triangular incisions on the front and back ends. Grave goods such as mirrors, iron swords, and iron halberds were reportedly found, although their details remain unclear. The sarcophagus has been preserved and is Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property. The original sarcophagus is displayed at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while a replica is exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|qq=|1=Osaka Ibaraki High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441161}}. == designation1 == === Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 === * Tangible designation1: ** Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun (archaeological artifact) - Preserved at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 List] (Osaka Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] ([[:en:Kanan, Osaka|Kanan, Osaka]], Minamikawachi District) - Preserves and exhibits the original sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|qq=|1=Osaka Ibaraki High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441161}} ([[:en:Ibaraki, Osaka|Ibaraki, Osaka]], Shinjōchō) - Displays a replica of the sarcophagus. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Local government publications: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=高槻市史 第6巻 考古編 (Takatsuki City History, Volume 6, Archaeological Edition)|year=1973|publisher=Takatsuki City|pages=59–60}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4900 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要9 (Summary of Excavations, No. 9)]|year=1985|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|page=18}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4644 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要13 (Summary of Excavations, No. 13)]|year=1989|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=19–23}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/5175 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要15 (Summary of Excavations, No. 15)]|year=1991|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=20–26}} * Other sources: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本歴史地名大系 28 大阪府の地名 (Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place names, Volume 28, Osaka)|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本古墳大辞典 (Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun)|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banzan Kofun|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|前塚古墳 (高槻市)|version=93995654|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takatsuki]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Maetsuka Kofun]] [[ja:前塚古墳 (高槻市)]] [[ja:前塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takatsuki]] [[zh:前塚古墳]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q11397093}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11397093]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Maezuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maezuka Kofun (Takatsuki), zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the tumulus | part_of = |location = Okamoto-chō 33 (Azatsuka-no-Mae), [[:en:Takatsuki|Takatsuki, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] |coordinates = {{Coord|34.852419|N|135.592597|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Kofun length: 94 m |built = 5th century |burial = Unknown (contains a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped stone coffin|qq=}} made of tuff) |Interred_individual = Unknown |burial goods = Grave goods, haniwa fragments |Historic Site = None |designation1 = Sarcophagus (Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 cultural property) |notes = |map = Japan Osaka |map_dot_label = Maezuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|qid=Q11480883}} }} '''Maezuka Kofun''' (前塚古墳) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Takatsuki|Takatsuki, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == The Maezuka Kofun was constructed at the southern end of the Bentenyama Hills in northern Takatsuki City, Osaka Prefecture. Over time, the mound has been partially leveled due to agricultural activities, and the surrounding moat has been interrupted by the construction of a road on the south side. Archaeological investigations have uncovered burial goods and remains of the tumulus, including a sarcophagus unearthed during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a short front section (a defining feature of this type of kofun) oriented westward. Fragments of haniwa, including cylindrical, lid-shaped, and house-shaped examples, have been found on the surface of the mound. The surrounding moat is 10–17 meters wide, and the total length of the kofun, including the moat, is estimated to be 120 meters. Although the specific structure of the burial facility is unclear, it is known that a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped stone coffin|qq=}} made of tuff was found inside, along with grave goods. Based on the haniwa fragments and sarcophagus design, the Maezuka Kofun is estimated to have been built during the 5th century, placing it in the middleof the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Previously, it was believed to be an {{ill|Baizuka|lt=accompanying mound|ja|陪塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} of the nearby [[:en:Imashirozuka Kofun|Imashirozuka Kofun]], but current research classifies it as an earlier kofun. The sarcophagus was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property in 1974. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length (including moat): 120 meters * Kofun length: 94 meters * Rear section (circular): ** Diameter: 69 meters * Front section (rectangular): ** Length: 25 meters ** Width: 25 meters (estimated) The construction plan shows similarities to the nearby {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banzan Kofun|qq=}} in Takatsuki City. The surrounding moat mirrors the type of the tumulus, measuring 16 meters wide on the rear side and 10 meters on the front side. == burial == [[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun
Designated as an Osaka Prefecture cultural property}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] The burial facility's structure is unknown, but it contained a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped stone coffin|qq=}}, which was exposed and discovered during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The sarcophagus, made of tuff, measures approximately 2 meters in length, 0.6 meters in width, and 0.8 meters in height. Its lid features a curved type (so-called "Inrō-lid"), with rope-like protrusions on each side and triangular incisions on the front and back ends. Grave goods such as mirrors, iron swords, and iron halberds were reportedly found, although their details remain unclear. The sarcophagus has been preserved and is Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property. The original sarcophagus is displayed at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while a replica is exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|qq=|1=Osaka Ibaraki High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441161}}. == designation1 == === Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 === * Tangible designation1: ** Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun (archaeological artifact) - Preserved at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 List] (Osaka Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] ([[:en:Kanan, Osaka|Kanan, Osaka]], Minamikawachi District) - Preserves and exhibits the original sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|qq=|1=Osaka Ibaraki High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441161}} ([[:en:Ibaraki, Osaka|Ibaraki, Osaka]], Shinjōchō) - Displays a replica of the sarcophagus. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Local government publications: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=高槻市史 第6巻 考古編 (Takatsuki City History, Volume 6, Archaeological Edition)|year=1973|publisher=Takatsuki City|pages=59–60}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4900 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要9 (Summary of Excavations, No. 9)]|year=1985|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|page=18}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4644 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要13 (Summary of Excavations, No. 13)]|year=1989|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=19–23}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/5175 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要15 (Summary of Excavations, No. 15)]|year=1991|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=20–26}} * Other sources: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本歴史地名大系 28 大阪府の地名 (Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place names, Volume 28, Osaka)|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本古墳大辞典 (Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun)|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banzan Kofun|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|前塚古墳 (高槻市)|version=93995654|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takatsuki]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:前塚古墳 (高槻市)]] [[ja:前塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takatsuki]] [[zh:前塚古墳]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q11397093}}

Preserves and exhibits the original sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|qq=|1=Osaka Ibaraki High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441161}} ([[:en:Ibaraki, Osaka|Ibaraki, Osaka]], Shinjōchō) - Displays a replica of the sarcophagus. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Local government publications: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=高槻市史 第6巻 考古編 (Takatsuki City History, Volume 6, Archaeological Edition)|year=1973|publisher=Takatsuki City|pages=59–60}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4900 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要9 (Summary of Excavations, No. 9)]|year=1985|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|page=18}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4644 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要13 (Summary of Excavations, No. 13)]|year=1989|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=19–23}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/5175 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要15 (Summary of Excavations, No. 15)]|year=1991|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=20–26}} * Other sources: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本歴史地名大系 28 大阪府の地名 (Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place names, Volume 28, Osaka)|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本古墳大辞典 (Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun)|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banzan Kofun|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|前塚古墳 (高槻市)|version=93995654|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takatsuki]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:前塚古墳 (高槻市)]] [[ja:前塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takatsuki]] [[zh:前塚古墳]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q11397093}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11397093]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Maezuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maezuka Kofun (Takatsuki), zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the tumulus | part_of = |location = Okamoto-chō 33 (Azatsuka-no-Mae), [[:en:Takatsuki|Takatsuki, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] |coordinates = {{Coord|34.852419|N|135.592597|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Kofun length: 94 m |built = 5th century |burial = Unknown (contains a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of tuff) |Interred_individual = Unknown |burial goods = Grave goods, haniwa fragments |Historic Site = None |designation1 = Sarcophagus (Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 cultural property) |notes = |map = Japan Osaka |map_dot_label = Maezuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|qid=Q11480883}} }} '''Maezuka Kofun''' (前塚古墳) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Takatsuki|Takatsuki, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == The Maezuka Kofun was constructed at the southern end of the Bentenyama Hills in northern Takatsuki City, Osaka Prefecture. Over time, the mound has been partially leveled due to agricultural activities, and the surrounding moat has been interrupted by the construction of a road on the south side. Archaeological investigations have uncovered burial goods and remains of the tumulus, including a sarcophagus unearthed during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a short front section (a defining feature of this type of kofun) oriented westward. Fragments of haniwa, including cylindrical, lid-shaped, and house-shaped examples, have been found on the surface of the mound. The surrounding moat is 10–17 meters wide, and the total length of the kofun, including the moat, is estimated to be 120 meters. Although the specific structure of the burial facility is unclear, it is known that a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of tuff was found inside, along with grave goods. Based on the haniwa fragments and sarcophagus design, the Maezuka Kofun is estimated to have been built during the 5th century, placing it in the middleof the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Previously, it was believed to be an {{ill|Baizuka|lt=accompanying mound|ja|陪塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} of the nearby [[:en:Imashirozuka Kofun|Imashirozuka Kofun]], but current research classifies it as an earlier kofun. The sarcophagus was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property in 1974. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length (including moat): 120 meters * Kofun length: 94 meters * Rear section (circular): ** Diameter: 69 meters * Front section (rectangular): ** Length: 25 meters ** Width: 25 meters (estimated) The construction plan shows similarities to the nearby {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banzan Kofun|qq=}} in Takatsuki City. The surrounding moat mirrors the type of the tumulus, measuring 16 meters wide on the rear side and 10 meters on the front side. == burial == [[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun
Designated as an Osaka Prefecture cultural property}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] The burial facility's structure is unknown, but it contained a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, which was exposed and discovered during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The sarcophagus, made of tuff, measures approximately 2 meters in length, 0.6 meters in width, and 0.8 meters in height. Its lid features a curved type (so-called "Inrō-lid"), with rope-like protrusions on each side and triangular incisions on the front and back ends. Grave goods such as mirrors, iron swords, and iron halberds were reportedly found, although their details remain unclear. The sarcophagus has been preserved and is Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property. The original sarcophagus is displayed at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while a replica is exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|qq=|1=Osaka Ibaraki High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441161}}. == designation1 == === Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 === * Tangible designation1: ** Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun (archaeological artifact) - Preserved at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 List] (Osaka Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] ([[:en:Kanan, Osaka|Kanan, Osaka]], Minamikawachi District) - Preserves and exhibits the original sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|qq=|1=Osaka Ibaraki High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441161}} ([[:en:Ibaraki, Osaka|Ibaraki, Osaka]], Shinjōchō) - Displays a replica of the sarcophagus. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Local government publications: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=高槻市史 第6巻 考古編 (Takatsuki City History, Volume 6, Archaeological Edition)|year=1973|publisher=Takatsuki City|pages=59–60}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4900 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要9 (Summary of Excavations, No. 9)]|year=1985|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|page=18}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4644 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要13 (Summary of Excavations, No. 13)]|year=1989|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=19–23}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/5175 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要15 (Summary of Excavations, No. 15)]|year=1991|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=20–26}} * Other sources: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本歴史地名大系 28 大阪府の地名 (Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place names, Volume 28, Osaka)|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本古墳大辞典 (Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun)|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banzan Kofun|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|前塚古墳 (高槻市)|version=93995654|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takatsuki]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:前塚古墳 (高槻市)]] [[ja:前塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takatsuki]] [[zh:前塚古墳]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q11397093}}

This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Maezuka Kofun |image = [[File:Maezuka Kofun (Takatsuki), zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the tumulus | part_of = |location = Okamoto-chō 33 (Azatsuka-no-Mae), [[:en:Takatsuki|Takatsuki, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] |coordinates = {{Coord|34.852419|N|135.592597|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Kofun length: 94 m |built = 5th century |burial = Unknown (contains a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of tuff) |Interred_individual = Unknown |burial goods = Grave goods, haniwa fragments |Historic Site = None |designation1 = Sarcophagus (Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 cultural property) |notes = |map = Japan Osaka |map_dot_label = Maezuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|qid=Q11480883}} }} '''Maezuka Kofun''' (前塚古墳) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Takatsuki|Takatsuki, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == The Maezuka Kofun was constructed at the southern end of the Bentenyama Hills in northern Takatsuki City, Osaka Prefecture. Over time, the mound has been partially leveled due to agricultural activities, and the surrounding moat has been interrupted by the construction of a road on the south side. Archaeological investigations have uncovered burial goods and remains of the tumulus, including a sarcophagus unearthed during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a short front section (a defining feature of this type of kofun) oriented westward. Fragments of haniwa, including cylindrical, lid-shaped, and house-shaped examples, have been found on the surface of the mound. The surrounding moat is 10–17 meters wide, and the total length of the kofun, including the moat, is estimated to be 120 meters. Although the specific structure of the burial facility is unclear, it is known that a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of tuff was found inside, along with grave goods. Based on the haniwa fragments and sarcophagus design, the Maezuka Kofun is estimated to have been built during the 5th century, placing it in the middleof the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Previously, it was believed to be an {{ill|Baizuka|lt=accompanying mound|ja|陪塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} of the nearby [[:en:Imashirozuka Kofun|Imashirozuka Kofun]], but current research classifies it as an earlier kofun. The sarcophagus was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property in 1974. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length (including moat): 120 meters * Kofun length: 94 meters * Rear section (circular): ** Diameter: 69 meters * Front section (rectangular): ** Length: 25 meters ** Width: 25 meters (estimated) The construction plan shows similarities to the nearby {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banzan Kofun|qq=}} in Takatsuki City. The surrounding moat mirrors the type of the tumulus, measuring 16 meters wide on the rear side and 10 meters on the front side. == burial == [[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun
Designated as an Osaka Prefecture cultural property}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] The burial facility's structure is unknown, but it contained a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, which was exposed and discovered during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The sarcophagus, made of tuff, measures approximately 2 meters in length, 0.6 meters in width, and 0.8 meters in height. Its lid features a curved type (so-called "Inrō-lid"), with rope-like protrusions on each side and triangular incisions on the front and back ends. Grave goods such as mirrors, iron swords, and iron halberds were reportedly found, although their details remain unclear. The sarcophagus has been preserved and is Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property. The original sarcophagus is displayed at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while a replica is exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|qq=|1=Osaka Ibaraki High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441161}}. == designation1 == === Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 === * Tangible designation1: ** Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun (archaeological artifact) - Preserved at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 List] (Osaka Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] ([[:en:Kanan, Osaka|Kanan, Osaka]], Minamikawachi District) - Preserves and exhibits the original sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|qq=|1=Osaka Ibaraki High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441161}} ([[:en:Ibaraki, Osaka|Ibaraki, Osaka]], Shinjōchō) - Displays a replica of the sarcophagus. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Local government publications: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=高槻市史 第6巻 考古編 (Takatsuki City History, Volume 6, Archaeological Edition)|year=1973|publisher=Takatsuki City|pages=59–60}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4900 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要9 (Summary of Excavations, No. 9)]|year=1985|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|page=18}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/4644 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要13 (Summary of Excavations, No. 13)]|year=1989|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=19–23}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search/item/5175 嶋上郡衙跡他関連遺跡発掘調査概要15 (Summary of Excavations, No. 15)]|year=1991|publisher=Takatsuki City Board of Education|pages=20–26}} * Other sources: ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本歴史地名大系 28 大阪府の地名 (Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place names, Volume 28, Osaka)|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|title=日本古墳大辞典 (Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun)|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banzan Kofun|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|前塚古墳 (高槻市)|version=93995654|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takatsuki]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:前塚古墳 (高槻市)]] [[ja:前塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takatsuki]] [[zh:前塚古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11397093}}

The surrounding moat mirrors the type of the tumulus, measuring 16 meters wide on the rear side and 10 meters on the front side. == burial == [[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun
Designated as an Osaka Prefecture cultural property}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] The burial facility's structure is unknown, but it contained a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, which was exposed and discovered during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The sarcophagus, made of tuff, measures approximately 2 meters in length, 0.6 meters in width, and 0.8 meters in height. Its lid features a curved type (so-called "Inrō-lid"), with rope-like protrusions on each side and triangular incisions on the front and back ends. Grave goods such as mirrors, iron swords, and iron halberds were reportedly found, although their details remain unclear. The sarcophagus has been preserved and is Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property. The original sarcophagus is displayed at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while a replica is exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|qq=|1=Osaka Ibaraki High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441161}}. == designation1 == === Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 === * Tangible designation1: ** Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun (archaeological artifact) - Preserved at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]]. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 List] (Osaka Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] ([[:en:Kanan, Osaka|Kanan, Osaka]], Minamikawachi District) - Preserves and exhibits the original sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun. * {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|qq=|1=Osaka Ibaraki High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441161}} ([[:en:Ibaraki, Osaka|Ibaraki, Osaka]], Shinjōchō) - Full view of the tumulus | part_of = |location = Okamoto-chō 33 (Azatsuka-no-Mae), [[:en:Takatsuki|Takatsuki, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] |coordinates = {{Coord|34.852419|N|135.592597|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Kofun length: 94 m |built = 5th century |burial = Unknown (contains a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of tuff) |Interred_individual = Unknown |burial goods = Grave goods, haniwa fragments |Historic Site = None |designation1 = Sarcophagus (Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 cultural property) |notes = |map = Japan Osaka |map_dot_label = Maezuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|WD=Q11480883}} }} '''Maezuka Kofun''' (前塚古墳) is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Takatsuki|Takatsuki, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture. The tumulus is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == The Maezuka Kofun was constructed at the southern end of the Bentenyama Hills in northern Takatsuki City, Osaka Prefecture. Over time, the mound has been partially leveled due to agricultural activities, and the surrounding moat has been interrupted by the construction of a road on the south side. Archaeological investigations have uncovered burial goods and remains of the tumulus, including a sarcophagus unearthed during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The tumulus is scallop-shaped, with a short front section (a defining feature of this type of kofun) oriented westward. Fragments of haniwa, including cylindrical, lid-shaped, and house-shaped examples, have been found on the surface of the mound. The surrounding moat is 10–17 meters wide, and the total length of the kofun, including the moat, is estimated to be 120 meters. Although the specific structure of the burial facility is unclear, it is known that a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of tuff was found inside, along with grave goods. Based on the haniwa fragments and sarcophagus design, the Maezuka Kofun is estimated to have been built during the 5th century, placing it in the middleof the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Previously, it was believed to be an {{ill|Baizuka|lt=accompanying mound|ja|陪塚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} of the nearby [[:en:Imashirozuka Kofun|Imashirozuka Kofun]], but current research classifies it as an earlier kofun. The sarcophagus was Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property in 1974. == area == The area of the tumulus are as follows: * Total length (including moat): 120 meters * Kofun length: 94 meters * Rear section (circular): ** Diameter: 69 meters * Front section (rectangular): ** Length: 25 meters ** Width: 25 meters (estimated) The construction plan shows similarities to the nearby {{ill|Banzan Kofun|ja|番山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Banzan Kofun|qq=}} in Takatsuki City. The surrounding moat mirrors the type of the tumulus, measuring 16 meters wide on the rear side and 10 meters on the front side. == burial == [[File:前塚古墳出土 長持形石棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun
Designated as an Osaka Prefecture cultural property}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}}]] The burial facility's structure is unknown, but it contained a {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, which was exposed and discovered during agricultural activity in the Meiji period. The sarcophagus, made of tuff, measures approximately 2 meters in length, 0.6 meters in width, and 0.8 meters in height. Its lid features a curved type (so-called "Inrō-lid"), with rope-like protrusions on each side and triangular incisions on the front and back ends. Grave goods such as mirrors, iron swords, and iron halberds were reportedly found, although their details remain unclear. The sarcophagus has been preserved and is Historic Site1 as an Osaka Prefecture-Historic Site1 tangible cultural property. The original sarcophagus is displayed at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]], while a replica is exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|ja|大阪府立茨木高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Ibaraki High School|1=Osaka Ibaraki High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441161}}. == designation1 == === Osaka Prefecture Designated designation1 === * Tangible designation1: ** Nagamochi-type sarcophagus from Maezuka Kofun (archaeological artifact) - Preserved at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].
